programme.title: Fish or Die
programme.desc: The anglers travel to Milne Day off the eastern tip of Papua New Guinea, the rumoured home to huge schools of the giant trevally. The crew try to hook one on a fly rod

programme.title: Project Grizzly
programme.desc: Jeff Watsons unconventional hunting lessons fail to prepare Bob and Screech to hunt game in the wild - but he discovers they may be learning on their own

programme.title: Expedition Bigfoot
programme.desc: After coming closer than ever to proving the existence of Bigfoot in Oregon, the team continues their search in the mountains of Kentucky

programme.title: Man-Eating Super Squid
programme.desc: Documentary searching for the truth behind the tales of the kraken, a monstrous killer squid that some claim has been responsible for the deaths of fishermen at sea

programme.title: My Extreme Animal Phobia
programme.desc: Former wrestler Kurt comes to terms with his fear of bees, Tameeka combats her hatred of millipedes, and Shelly confronts a long-standing unease relating to sharks

programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps Ingrid, Anicka and Nikki, whose long-standing friendship is being threatened by the appalling behaviour of Ingrids cat, Milo

programme.title: Must Love Cats
programme.desc: John Fulton discovers how the comic-strip cat Garfield was born, what cat litter is made of, and whether a specimen with four ears has better than the average hearing

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: During a routine hunting licence check, Game Wardens Ben and Chelsea discover illegal drugs. And, trespassers get caught in a dangerous restricted area

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff and his team battle to save a puppy with broken ribs and internal bleeding. Meanwhile, Dr Baier attempts surgery he has never done before to treat a familys beloved rat

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: After 25 years in a tiny clinic, the team moves to a new building with twice the space. Dr Jeff also makes a special connection with a dog owner

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Mariah and Spencer rescue a dog with severe injuries, a parolee gets arrested and a couple hope to find a friend for their bulldog

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Tia takes in a pit bull that was rescued from awful conditions in Kuwait. Meanwhile, when a stray dog arrives at Villalobos, Earl is determined to find the owner

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy visits a house divided, with four cats always fighting. He also helps out at a cattery where territorial issues have two cats to live in isolation for years

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy comes to the aid of a mother and daughter whose feline companion Missy is determined to kill their other cat Fluffy

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson goes behind the scenes of the past series to revisit some of his favourite projects, including Dale Earnhardt Jrs arboral escape

programme.title: Meet the Sloths
programme.desc: Sensitive Linus hits a rocky patch when the nursery manager has to leave the sanctuary, and the resident scientist reveals which of the species is a natural born swimmer

programme.title: Meet the Sloths
programme.desc: A visit from a wild mother and baby almost ends in disaster, young Choloepus Tigger starts to display un-slothlike tendencies, and the resident scientist tries to find out what excites the females of the species

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Experts answer more questions about the animal kingdom, including what lions are saying when they roar and what leafcutter ants do with all the leaves they collect

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Why domestic cats have trouble getting down from trees and why kiwi birds lay monster-sized eggs compared to their size

programme.title: The Diamond Collar
programme.desc: James Giuliani and Lena Perrelli look for a site for their future animal sanctuary, but the trip is put on hold when officers call James to help rescue a dog in need

programme.title: The Diamond Collar
programme.desc: James Giuliani gets an unusual call to find a new home for a Peking duck. Meanwhile, Lena Perrelli prepares for her mothers surprise party and requires James assistance

programme.title: Alaska: Surviving the Last Frontier
programme.desc: Following four groups of ordinary people as they travel into the wilderness of Alaska, where they must find a new home in the inhospitable environment and deal with the threat posed by bears, glaciers and their own fears

programme.title: Tanked
programme.sub-title: Brace Yourself for Employee of the Month
programme.desc: The brothers-in-law create unique aquariums for a luxury apartment complex and a knee brace company, and Wayde King hopes to galvanise his employees by holding a special contest

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Brett Raymer and Wayde King visit an inn in Ojai, California, to work on an aquarium built into the trunk of an oak tree. A Halloween store in Las Vegas also requests a coffin tank

programme.title: Must Love Cats
programme.desc: John meets detectives who help to find lost cats, and discovers how some felines are able to overcome tough injuries thanks to animal physical therapy methods

programme.title: Ninja Sharks
programme.desc: When fish huddle together for protection, a hungry shark needs to get creative. The Thresher Shark knows just the trick

programme.desc: Phobia expert Dr Robin Zasio comes to the aid of Katie, who is afraid of spiders and will go out of her way to secure every nook and cranny against them. The programme also features Andrews fear of cockroaches and Jackies fear of butterflies

programme.desc: A perpetually angry cat causes self-inflicted wounds to its tail and body. Meanwhile, Samantha and Adams feline Gizmo goes on the rampage. Jackson Galaxy comes to their aid

programme.title: Preposterous Pets
programme.desc: A young woman on the west coast of America experiences the night life with a pair of tamandua anteaters. Plus, the story of a couple who share their home with hippos

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Former Guns N Roses guitarist DJ Ashba asks Brett and Wayde to build him a creepy skull-themed aquarium

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: The officers come to the rescue of six hungry horses, and try to rehouse an elderly grey mare called Grandma

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: After a string of bad luck with some of the parolees, Tia hopes new arrival Richard will prove to be a valuable asset. The team rushes to save a mutilated pup

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy works with a Bengal that keeps spraying on every piece of furniture in the house. Plus, an energetic cat threatens to harm a toddler

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy works with a mother and daughter whose cats are having a territorial spraying match. He also meets with trouble-making cat Bowie, who has been given his last chance

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: The wardens investigate the death of a deer and reports of fawns being illegally kept as pets. A suspicious car is stopped in Red River County

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.sub-title: The Dog Who Won Jeffs Heart
programme.desc: A client brings in an unusual pet who is fast outgrowing his enclosure. Dr Jeff treats a cat with several serious - and mysterious - injuries

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Treehouse visionary Alain Laurens joins Pete on his travels through France, where he shares his fantastical structures that redefine what a treehouse can be

programme.title: Ninja Sharks
programme.desc: When fish huddle together for protection, a hungry shark needs to get creative. The Thresher Shark knows just the trick

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: As spring draws to a close, Otto and August set to work on a large-scale plumbing project, and Atz Sr searches for predators threatening the Kilcher property

programme.title: Alaska Gold Diggers
programme.desc: A power struggle erupts in Gold Run between SJ and John over mining locations - and there seems to be no easy solution for them to set their differences aside

programme.title: The Haunted
programme.sub-title: The House of the Rising Dead
programme.desc: A woman rebuilding her mansion following hurricane Katrina feels the presence of another in her historic home

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: Marty, Matt and Misty Raney help the Russell family, who are struggling on a failing homestead. Food is low, they do not know how to hunt and their cabin is collapsing

programme.title: My Extreme Animal Phobia
programme.desc: A crippling fear of bats prevents Tom from leaving the house at night, Roxanne will not enter the bathroom by herself for fear of rats, and Laura strives hard to avoid cockroaches

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Out on a Limb
programme.desc: Pete Nelson and his team build an Alpine-style treehouse that is a perfect fit to the Christmas season

programme.title: Animal Cops San Francisco
programme.desc: A dead puppy is found chained up in an abandoned house, and a cat hoarder is encouraged to give up some pets

programme.title: Animal Cops San Francisco
programme.desc: Officer Kent rescues a neglected puppy whose embedded collar has caused a festering wound. Plus, a troubled young woman steals a golden chow

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy revisits cat owners from previous shows to recap on their stories, and finds out how they and their cats have done in the intervening period

programme.title: Catfishin Kings
programme.desc: The teams brave treacherous lake conditions to show their Texas noodlin

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Comedian Bill Engvall requests a Zen-like fish tank for his new office in Hollywood. But a firm weight restriction throws a spanner in the works

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: The SPCA seizes a hoard of sick animals, including several roosters, a goat and a pregnant cat. Plus, investigator Scott Wernick confiscates numerous dogs that have been left to fend for themselves

programme.title: The Great British Year
programme.desc: Documentary following British wildlife through the seasons. Spring marks a series of beginnings, as trees explode with blossom and mornings fill with the chorus of birdsong. Long-tailed tits frantically build nests, a stoat mother hunts rabbits to feed her playful young, while in the oceans, seahorses sway to a graceful courtship dance. As spring becomes summer, guillemot chicks leap from their cliffs to begin life at sea. Narrated by Joseph Fiennes

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: At the behest of a local businessman, unlikely manhunter Hetty fearlessly sets out in search of the thugs responsible for attacking Asian businessmen. She soon discovers that every victim has been a restaurant owner, but then Hetty herself becomes a target of the gang involved

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Chairman Leonard Sachs welcomes Georgia Brown, Barry Cryer, Katinka Seiner, John Inman, Barry Howard, Marie-Ange Brillet and the Three Hurricanes to the stage of the City Varieties Theatre, Leeds. First shown in 1975

programme.title: Travels with Pevsner
programme.desc: Germaine Greer explores the architecture of Warwickshire with Nikolaus Pevsners Buildings of England as her guide, with visits to Baddesley Clinton Hall and Warwick Castle

programme.title: One Hundred Great Paintings
programme.sub-title: Love: Gainsborough - Mr and Mrs Andrews
programme.desc: Edwin Mullins examinies Thomas Gainsboroughs 1750 work Mr and Mrs Andrews, which combines a double portrait of the newly married couple with a wide view of their English country estate

programme.title: Rebuilding Notre-Dame
programme.desc: On December 7, 2024, the cathedral of Notre-Dame de Paris reopened five years after the devastating fire that ravaged the world-famous gothic masterpiece. Here, Lucy Worsley follows the final stages of the restoration work, focusing on the teams battling to ensure the complex roof and spire were ready for the public reopening

programme.title: Krapps Last Tape
programme.desc: A 69-year-old man sits alone on his birthday and listens to his taped memoirs, particularly those recalling past loves. Harold Pinters performance at the Royal Court Theatre of Samuel Becketts one-act play, which focuses on mortality, creativity and memory and chronicles a tussle between present and past

programme.title: Searching for Sam: Adrian Dunbar on Samuel Beckett
programme.desc: Thirty years after the playwrights death in Paris at the age of 83, Line of Duty star Adrian Dunbar explores the life and work of Samuel Beckett to find out about the man behind Waiting for Godot. Although born and raised in Dublin, Ireland, he was a resident of Paris for most of his adult life, writing in both French and English, and was awarded the 1969 Nobel Prize in Literature for his work

programme.title: Julian Lloyd Webber with the London Philharmonic Orchestra
programme.desc: Robert Walker introduces a performance of Elgars Cello Concerto in E minor, with Yehudi Menuhin conducting cellist Julian Lloyd Webber and the London Philharmonic Orchestra at the 1987 Harrogate International Festival

programme.title: BBC Proms
programme.desc: A 2022 concert recorded at the Royal Albert Hall in which the multi award-winning star of stage and screen is backed by the BBC Concert Orchestra as she pays tribute to the female vocalists who inspired her, including Nina Simone, Shirley Bassey, Billie Holiday and Gladys Knight. Andi Oliver presents, and is joined by guests Clarke Peters and Ayanna Witter-Johnson

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the fauna of Africa, home to the greatest wildlife gatherings on Earth. But even in this land of plenty, animals face huge challenges. At its heart is a vast tropical rainforest full of life, where young chimpanzees learn how to use tools to make the most of the jungles riches. With knowledge passed down from generation to generation, they can access the best forest foods

programme.title: Travels with Pevsner
programme.desc: Germaine Greer explores the architecture of Warwickshire with Nikolaus Pevsners Buildings of England as her guide, with visits to Baddesley Clinton Hall and Warwick Castle

programme.title: Rebuilding Notre-Dame
programme.desc: On December 7, 2024, the cathedral of Notre-Dame de Paris reopened five years after the devastating fire that ravaged the world-famous gothic masterpiece. Here, Lucy Worsley follows the final stages of the restoration work, focusing on the teams battling to ensure the complex roof and spire were ready for the public reopening

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the fauna of Africa, home to the greatest wildlife gatherings on Earth. But even in this land of plenty, animals face huge challenges. At its heart is a vast tropical rainforest full of life, where young chimpanzees learn how to use tools to make the most of the jungles riches. With knowledge passed down from generation to generation, they can access the best forest foods

programme.title: The Latest Secrets of Hieroglyphs
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the latest research into the creation of the ancient Egyptians written language. The programme follows Egyptologists as they unlock the texts inscribed inside a richly adorned tomb, revealing the beliefs and lives of the priests, scribes, painters, engravers and builders who created this grand funerary monument

programme.title: Call My Bluff
programme.desc: Robert Robinson hosts as Patrick Campbell, Joanna Lumley and Frank Kearton take on Frank Muir, Pauline Yates and Donald Churchill in the game of word definitions and deceptions. First broadcast in 1976

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against guests Annette Page, Richard Baker and Robin Ray. With guest musician Murray Perahia. First broadcast in 1979

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Maggie Aderin and the team return, beginning by exploring the progress of Nasas Artemis II mission, the first crewed journey back toward the Moon in more than 50 years. It is designed to carry astronauts around the far side and safely home again, marking the furthest human beings will have travelled into space.

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: How discoveries from the edge of the solar system transformed what was known about Pluto, thanks to the New Horizons space probe that took the first-ever close-up images of the dwarf planet. Pluto was once thought to be geologically dead, but the pictures revealed it to be an active world of stunning complexity, with mountains carved from ice, a nitrogen glacier that appeared to be moving and a recently active volcano. The data even led some scientists to speculate there may even be life on Pluto today

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: Contemporary artists and performers, alongside historians and curators,examine the creative renewal in literature, music and art in the aftermath of the Black Death, with survivors finding their voice, questioning authority and challenging status and class. Above all, writing in English was revived by works including Geoffrey Chaucers The Canterbury Tales, William Langlands satire The Vision of Piers Plowman and breakthrough works by women like the spirited pilgrim Margery Kempe

programme.title: The Secret Life of Waves
programme.desc: David Malone investigates the importance of ocean waves. He discovers they are not made of water, can travel sideways and their sound is created by bubbles, and examines the connection between the life cycles of waves and human beings

programme.title: The Latest Secrets of Hieroglyphs
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the latest research into the creation of the ancient Egyptians written language. The programme follows Egyptologists as they unlock the texts inscribed inside a richly adorned tomb, revealing the beliefs and lives of the priests, scribes, painters, engravers and builders who created this grand funerary monument

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: Contemporary artists and performers, alongside historians and curators,examine the creative renewal in literature, music and art in the aftermath of the Black Death, with survivors finding their voice, questioning authority and challenging status and class. Above all, writing in English was revived by works including Geoffrey Chaucers The Canterbury Tales, William Langlands satire The Vision of Piers Plowman and breakthrough works by women like the spirited pilgrim Margery Kempe

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Claudia is joined by Oscar-nominated star of Schindlers List, Ralph Fiennes, who is on the sofa to talk about his play Grace Pervades. Comedy actress Anna Faris returns to the world of Scary Movie for the next instalment in the spoof-horror franchise. Olivia Cooke, star of Amazon Primes The Girlfriend, talks about the upcoming series in the Game of Thrones prequel, House of the Dragon, and comedian Michelle de Swarte tells Claudia about her stand-up tour The Afters

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Dean Westmoreland is presented with pair of pointe shoes worn by former ballerina Julie Felix for her landmark performance at the Royal Opera House, and Charlotte Abbott fixes a model Range Rover that represents a big globetrotting adventure by a six-man military team. Chris Shaw takes on a fragile 1917 scrapbook documenting the rise of the groundbreaking Dick Kerr Ladies, the munitions factory football team who became global pioneers of the womens game

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Arsenal v Bournemouth at Emirates Stadium and Brentford v Everton at Gtech Community Stadium. A brace from Declan Rice helped the title-chasing Gunners to a 3-2 away win over Bournemouth in January and they will have had high hopes of picking up another three points. The Bees and Everton are both chasing European places and it was Brentford who came out on top in the reverse fixture, with Igor Thiago scoring a hat-trick in a 4-2 triumph. Plus, Burnley v Brighton and Hove Albion and Liverpool v Fulham

programme.title: Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg
programme.desc: Interviews with key politicians and public figures

programme.title: Puss in Boots (2011)
programme.desc: The swashbuckling feline joins forces with a cunning thief and a treacherous old friend to steal a magical golden goose from a giants castle. He must save the day when it transpires that their actions have unleashed a terrible danger on his home town. Animated comedy spin-off from the Shrek movies, with the voices of Antonio Banderas and Salma Hayek

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Pam Rhodes meets Christians expressing faith through hospitality and talks to the Archbishop of Wales, Cherry Vann, about why offering a welcome lies at the heart of Christianity

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Wakefield Trinity v Wigan Warriors (Kick-off 1.00pm). Coverage of the quarter-final at DIY Kitchens Stadium. Wakefield defeated Leeds Rhinos 24-14 here in the previous round and are looking to lift this trophy for the first time since 1964. Wigan have won this competition a record 21 times with the last of their triumphs coming two seasons ago, and they overcame Bradford Bulls 30-6 in round four

programme.title: Crookhaven
programme.desc: The Crooklings compete at the cutthroat trials, with the eyes of the crooked world watching. The Nameless spy resorts to desperate measures, and Jia leads the search for Amira

programme.title: Crookhaven
programme.desc: The Masquerade Ball reveals everyones true colours, an enemy within forces Gabriel to question everything, and Caspians plan starts to unravel, putting Penelope in grave danger

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local News from across Northern Ireland with Tara Mills and Declan Harvey

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: John Craven and Charlotte Smith head to the National Forest, to mark the planting of its 10-millionth tree. They discover how three decades of regeneration have transformed scarred mining land into a rich wildlife habitat, and meet people whose lives have been reshaped by the Forest, from a womens walking group to craftspeople building sustainable livelihoods from woodland materials. In Devon, Adam Henson meets a young farming couple reviving the practice of traditional woodland grazing

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the hidden world of animals making a life for themselves in a small urban garden in Bristol. A young male fox treats the garden as the hub of his hundred-garden territory, a young blue tit, driven to the city by hunger, battles aggressive goldfinches, a female hedgehog wakes from hibernation, and a pond is the focus for frog mating season

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce returns to Cromford Mills in Derbyshire, where visitors include a pilot who has constructed her own planes and brought along a wooden propeller from a biplane. Among the treasures being valued are a signed photograph of John F Kennedy and a pair of 300-year-old long johns, made for an Emperor of Japan. Plus, Eric Knowles takes a look back over his 44 years on the show

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: A group hike up Scafell Pike brings buried emotions and romantic confusion to the surface. During an unforeseen storm, Mary finds herself caught in the middle of rising tensions between Mr Hayward and Mr Ryder. As their expedition takes a turn for the worse, Mary is faced with real danger, and the events of the day leave her feeling more conflicted than ever before

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: Mary returns to London, where she receives an unexpected offer and faces renewed pressure from Mrs Bennet to find a match. After a run-in with an old friend, and with Mr Hayward absent from London, Mary wonders what happiness could look like for her. Can she find the courage to finally stand up to her mother and forge a path of her own?

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: Carey gains full control of SO15 and releases Whitlock, leaving Figgis with no choice but to act. The Correction Inquiry hears a shocking revelation that puts Garland in the glare of the worlds media. E-Squadron goes on the offensive, and with few allies left to trust, sacrifice is inevitable - just how far is Rachel Carey willing to go to protect the truth?

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local News from across Northern Ireland with Tara Mills and Declan Harvey

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Chelsea v Manchester City at Stamford Bridge and Nottingham Forest v Aston Villa at The City Ground. City were unbeaten in their last 12 matches with Chelsea in all competitions, but the sides drew 1-1 in the reverse fixture at Etihad Stadium in January. Forest and the Villans were both in needs of points for differing reasons, with the hosts battling to avoid relegation and Unai Emerys men aiming to qualify for the Champions League. Plus, Crystal Palace v Newcastle United and Sunderland v Tottenham Hotspur

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Cows Stranded in a River, Poachers Tracked by Drone
programme.desc: In Co Down, dairy farmer Derek Geddis turns to a volunteer search and rescue team after three of his cattle go missing. When the animals are found stranded in a river, the farmers skills and the teams technology are put to the test in a delicate recovery mission. In Co Durham, a community support officers drone footage helps catch deer poachers in the act. His quick response and aerial evidence lead to a rare conviction. Plus, in north Wales, reporter Paul Colgan investigates how police and scientists are using dog DNA to trace the animals behind devastating livestock attacks

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: In Harthill, Scotland, Martel Maxwell encounters a house with a safety order attached to it, which means certain rooms cannot be accessed. Owain Wyn Evans heads to south Wales to see a sizeable unit with lots of development potential. Meanwhile, in Lincolnshire, Dion Dublin does all he can to escape the rain

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams of sisters compete at an antiques centre in Derbyshire, aided by Nick Hall and Izzie Balmer. Plus, Christina Trevanion meets a man who built his own cinema in his garden

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: Joanna Page presents a shopping challenge, where couples scour the UKs charity shops to see if they can make even more money at auction for the items they purchase, and raise money for their chosen causes. In Hastings, partners James and Elaine scour 15 charity shops, hunting for vintage gems and a hidden star buy before facing a tense, live online auction for charity

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: Interior designer Banjo Beale and his team of tradespeople transforms a family-run fish shop in Tobermory on the Isle of Mull. The job is a commission from Banjos friend Sally, who is the manager and wants to modernise the traditional business

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Richie Anderson helps a couple fulfil their forest school dream in Oxfordshire. He also meets an innovative artist who creates masterpieces on a bicycle-powered sewing machine

programme.title: I Made It at Market
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea revisits upholsterer Hayley and stone carver Richard to find out how they have built money-making businesses after leaving his business bootcamp

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which Lee, Nadine, Tom, Carol and Albert race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Tom Allen for the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local News from across Northern Ireland with Tara Mills and Declan Harvey

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ross and Vicki celebrate their stag and hen parties, Jasmine starts to feel part of the Slater family and Bea makes a decision about her love life

programme.title: Ronan Keatings Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Ronan completes this leg of his journey along Irelands Wild Atlantic in County Derry/Londonderry. He walks the 400-year-old walls of the city of Derry/Londonderry with comedian Diona Doherty. He also hits the farm to find out what it takes to be a competitive sheep herder, before heading for the windswept coastline of Castlerock to meet a skilled craft worker who builds traditional coracle boats by hand

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: TV presenter and keen gardener Katie Piper joins Diarmuid and Carol to visit three more gardens. The judges head to Colins surprising rustic garden in suburban Ballymena, before seeing how Adrienne has transformed two acres near the windswept Glenshane Pass in the foothills of the Sperrin Mountains. Their final stop is in Dromore, Co Down, where Julietta has created a garden designed with her young family in mind, including a wild meadow playground and a courtyard filled with edible plants

programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: Lisa tries to find a way of getting Stephen to talk to her. Amanda splits up with Dave before beginning the move to her new life

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local News from across Northern Ireland with Tara Mills and Declan Harvey

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Darragh MacIntyre reports on Donald Trumps hardline immigration policies, which have left an estimated 14 million undocumented immigrants are at risk of deportation and resulted in a sharp rise in arrests in US cities and protesters being shot dead on the streets of Minneapolis by federal agents

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Monty Don hosts, with guest panellists comedian Chris McCausland and journalist Helen Lewis joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the latest news

programme.title: Tinker Tailor Soldier Spy (2011)
programme.desc: George Smiley, the deputy to the head of British intelligence, is forced into retirement after an operation ends in disaster with the death of an MI6 agent. Smiley is later recruited by the government to return to duty and investigate the possibility of a Soviet mole in a high-ranking position within MI6. Cold War thriller based on John le Carres 1974 novel, starring an Oscar-nominated Gary Oldman, alongside Colin Firth, Tom Hardy, John Hurt, Benedict Cumberbatch, Toby Jones, Mark Strong, Ciaran Hinds and Roger Lloyd Pack

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: A young patient is reported to have had a seizure while out walking in a forest in Bingley, West Yorkshire - but paramedics Sam and Sophie are struggling to locate her. Meanwhile, crewmates Jordan and Josh are on their way to an 85-year-old woman with chest pains when they are diverted to a category one call for an unconscious male. However, they quickly realise the man in question is conscious and a regular caller to 999 who would be best seen at hospital - an offer he refuses

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Dead Birds of Prey Discovery Reveals Shocking Crime
programme.desc: On farmland in Wiltshire, investigators uncover one of Britains most shocking cases of bird crime, after a tip-off leads to a secret camera catching a suspect in the act. As teams sift through horrific evidence of dead raptors, the scale of what could be the UKs largest bird of prey persecution becomes clear. In Northern Ireland, reporter Paul Colgan joins the Mourne Mountain Rescue Team to discover the challenges they overcome to keep hill farmers safe in some of the countrys toughest terrain

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts encounters a layout he has never seen before, which is rare given his 20-plus years on the show. Dion Dublin encounters all weathers in Manchester and Leeds

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Part one of two. Tir Dhondy reveals the realities behind modern Las Vegas. The first episode explores the risk of crime faced by tourists and visits a pool party where massive screens show scores of live sports events from around the world so that partygoers can bet from the comfort of their sun lounger

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: Gemma and Lorcan throw a party, but Gemma finds one of her old personality quirks coming to the fore again as she struggles to maintain her identity amidst the intoxicating brew of Lorcans old-fashioned country charm. Callum makes a new friend in Gemmas brother David, but finds theres a catch. Comedy, with Rian Lennon, Ryan Dylan and Ele McKenzie

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: It is the day of Janet and Jonnys wedding, but the bride and groom are locked in the back of a van surrounded by a breakfast of dressed crabs and baked beans. While they ponder whether it was ever meant to be, Gaz comes up with a plan to rectify the situation - he and Donna will get married, but sign their friends names on the register

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Gaz reacts to the misery of Donna leaving him by binge eating. Jonny gets a job working in the pub, while his house receives a much-needed tidy, courtesy of Louises group of teenage delinquents - who provide Janet with an opportunity to work on developing that all-important maternal instinct

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet struggles to remember things, so Louise signs her up for a pub quiz to keep her mind active. However, she gets wrapped up in the competition and forgets her antenatal class - prompting Gaz to accompany Jonny in her place. Comedy, starring Sheridan Smith

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Gaz is run over by an ambulance, making Donna realise she still loves him. However, while concussed, he confesses to being the father of Janets baby - which sends a furious Jonny storming off to the maternity ward in search of answers. Meanwhile, Louise worries about her career prospects

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Jonny feels neglected as Janet focuses all her attention on the baby, and he resorts to desperate measures to get her to notice him. Donna flaunts her new-found financial success, but refuses to share the wealth with her friends, while Louise cannot understand why the others have no time for her. Comedy, starring Ralf Little

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jasmine receives a visitor, Nicola is left frustrated, and Ross and Harvey hatch a plan

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tensions are high as Jasmines trial commences, George makes a declaration and Ross grows paranoid

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jasmine faces a crucial decision, Penny confronts Nicola, and Ross and Vicki discuss their future

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The verdict in Jasmines trial is revealed, Vicki shares her hesitations, and Elaine and Ian hold their campaign launch events

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey and the team head to Twickenham to help the Allan family tackle their overflowing home. Mum Sharon has ADHD and thrives when things have a place - but standards have slipped and the house has never had a proper clear-out. Daughter Holly, who is autistic, finds comfort in routine and familiar objects and feels overwhelmed by the clutter. As the team snoop around, they soon discover part of the problem - dad Brians secret gadget-buying habit

programme.title: Tenet (2020)
programme.desc: A troubleshooter is hired for a high-stakes mission to prevent armageddon. As he navigates through a world of espionage, Protagonist uncovers a shadowy organisation with the ability to manipulate time. Racing against the clock, whichever way its ticking, he must master the concept of inversion to thwart the catastrophic future. Christopher Nolans mind-bending fantasy action thriller, starring John David Washington, Kenneth Branagh and Elizabeth Debicki

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: The case of Michaella McCollum, a teenager from Northern Ireland who formed a relationship with a drug trafficker during her first trip abroad in Ibiza. He persuaded her to assist him in smuggling drugs for his cartel contacts, and just a few weeks later, she was arrested at Lima airport in Peru with more than a million pounds worth of cocaine in her suitcase

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Young offender Michaella McCollum, who became infamous worldwide as one half of the Peru Two, offers her account of how she came to be arrested, tried and jailed in one of the most notorious maximum security prisons in Peru. She recounts how she and fellow would-be mule Melissa Reid were tasked with pretending to be tourists at Machu Picchu, before walking 12kg of cocaine into Jorge Chávez International Airport in August 2013

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Part two of two. Tir Dhondy goes further into the underbelly of Las Vegas to investigate how the reinvention of the city to pull in a new generation of visitors is affecting locals

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: Part one of two. After a video featuring a female prison officer and an inmate having sex inside HMP Wandsworth goes viral, journalist Mobeen Azhar begins an investigation into what happened and why. A year of twists, turns and damning revelations pulls back the curtain on a dangerous wider world of coercion, corruption and contraband thriving behind bars

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: Part two of two. Mobeen Azhars investigation into prison corruption uncovers the murder of a former officer who had been involved in exposing an illicit relationship. When news breaks of a prison governor being jailed for a relationship with a drug-dealing gang boss, Mobeen becomes determined to uncover the true extent of criminal influence inside our prisons

programme.title: Press X to Continue
programme.desc: Steffan Powell chats with Elz the Witch, Inel Tomlinson and Glenn Moore about which eight games they would take to a mythical desert island

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall explores the world of parasites, including tapeworms, mosquitoes and cuckoos, investigating the skills they use to exploit their hosts

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall explores dangerous wildlife in Venezuela, including green anacondas, vampire bats and scolopendras - giant venomous centipedes

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May try to impress Oxford residents by building an electric car, and refuse to let an unenthusiastic response deter them from sitting in on the machines crash-test before handing it over to the Stigs environmentally friendly cousin. Actor Michael Sheen is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car, and Jeremy pits the Corvette ZR1 against the Audi R8 V10

programme.title: Louis Therouxs LA Stories
programme.desc: The intrepid reporter explores different aspects of Los Angeles, beginning by meeting the characters whose lives revolve around the citys neglected and feral dogs. He goes on patrol with a man on a mission to rescue some of the thousands of stray and ferocious pit bulls roaming the streets and meets hard-pressed staff at LAs largest animal shelter. Louis also encounters training gurus who claim to be able to take the ghetto out of the dog once it has been removed from that environment

programme.title: Louis Therouxs LA Stories
programme.desc: Louis explores the American attitude to death as he visits Cedars-Sinai Medical Centre in West Hollywood, where he meets people battling against serious illnesses. He talks to patients and their families as they face whether to accept death and die in relative comfort, or to gamble on further, possibly painful treatments to prolong their lives, and meets the doctors whose job it is to guide people through the traumatic process

programme.title: Louis Therouxs LA Stories
programme.desc: California has some of the toughest regulations controlling sex offenders anywhere in America - after leaving prison they can spend years on parole being tagged and tracked and a lifetime on an online register. In his final film exploring different aspects of life in Los Angeles, Louis Theroux visits hostels to talk to parolees as well as the people charged with monitoring them, and asks whether the restrictions keep society safer and when, if ever, it is right to give sex offenders a second chance

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Michaella explains she was faced with a dilemma - stick to her story about being forced to carry the drugs and face 15 years in jail, or come clean and break her mothers heart

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Michaella discusses how she claimed to have been forced into smuggling in the hope of getting a reduced sentence, only to find herself spending the first of what would be many days behind bars in the cells of the polices anti-narcotics division. However, her story soon caught the attention of the international press, with reporters around the world desperate for the inside scoop

programme.title: Louis Therouxs LA Stories
programme.desc: Louis explores the American attitude to death as he visits Cedars-Sinai Medical Centre in West Hollywood, where he meets people battling against serious illnesses. He talks to patients and their families as they face whether to accept death and die in relative comfort, or to gamble on further, possibly painful treatments to prolong their lives, and meets the doctors whose job it is to guide people through the traumatic process

programme.title: Louis Therouxs LA Stories
programme.desc: California has some of the toughest regulations controlling sex offenders anywhere in America - after leaving prison they can spend years on parole being tagged and tracked and a lifetime on an online register. In his final film exploring different aspects of life in Los Angeles, Louis Theroux visits hostels to talk to parolees as well as the people charged with monitoring them, and asks whether the restrictions keep society safer and when, if ever, it is right to give sex offenders a second chance

programme.title: Press X to Continue
programme.desc: Steffan Powell is joined by SunPi, Mr Midas and Shay Thompson to talk through whats going on with online game stores at Xbox, Playstation and Nintendo

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: In the early hours of a Saturday morning a woman calls 999 to report that she had been attacked and raped by a stranger in a public phone box in Birmingham. Crime scene co-ordinator Jo Ward directs a detailed examination of the phone box for any evidence that may help police identify the attacker, with an investigation being launched by West Midlands Polices specialist Rape and Serious Sexual Offences team, led by Detective Inspector Emma Watson

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: Carey and Pierson escape into a network of underground tunnels. Unarmed and with no back-up, Carey is horrified by what Pierson has been through, and the extent of the conspiracy that they are both now entangled in. Simons real identity is revealed as allies above ground piece together the truth, and Carey forges an uneasy alliance to bring down the forces truly responsible for Isaac Turners murder

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.sub-title: Easter: The Sights and Sounds of Spring
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor visit Bisterne Estate in Hampshires Avon Valley on Easter Sunday, where farmland and water meadows provide a vital home for lapwings and hares. Plus, author Robert Macfarlane shares an exclusive spring reading, and on his Cotswolds farm, Adam Henson restores a traditional hedgerow, just in time for nesting birds

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond visits Inveraray Castle in Argyll and Bute to look back on a devastating fire that gutted it half a century ago and hears how it was saved with help from local people. Cammy Wilson watches the action at the Stirling Bull Sales to find out why some animals fetch record prices, while Arlene Stuart revisits a story from the Landward archive, following in the footsteps of presenter Ken Rundle as he found out about a new farm diversification called agritourism. Rosie Morton heads to see farmers putting the value back into their Hebridean sheep fleeces

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: The team looks ahead to summer days in the garden, with Lizzie Schofield pruning and planting in her sitooterie area, while Brian Cunningham has a beginners guide to starting a productive vegetable plot. Lizzie reveals which varieties of tomatoes she has picked for 2026 and is also the first of the team to start work on her competition garden, based on planting that appeals to one of the senses

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents more highlights, with recipe by Anna Jones, Joe Wicks, Sabrina Ghayour and Georgina Hayden, while Stacey Dooley tucks into either her food heaven of food hell

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: London and South East England: Main and Dessert
programme.desc: The remaining three chefs from London and south-east England cook mains and desserts. Their creations are judged by veteran judge Aktar Islam. Dishes include 28-day dry aged duck inspired by 28 Days Later and a peanut and salted caramel parfait that serves as a tribute to the BAFTAs

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: London and South East England: Judging
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from London and south-east England must go head-to-head and cook their six-course menus again. Actor Simon Callow helps decide the winner from the two remaining chefs. Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts from the British Motor Museum in Warwickshire, where Richard Madley and Philip Serrell help two teams select items to take to auction

programme.title: Pilgrimage
programme.desc: The pilgrims are in the Scottish Borders on St Cuthberts Way in the Cheviots. It takes them on a strenuous route up Wideopen Hill to reach some spectacular views. Later, they head to Cuthberts Cave, in the Kelso Hills, and on the last day of their pilgrimage they follow the 5km walk across the tidal sands of the Pilgrims Way. Finally, they arrive at Holy Island and reflect on their journey

programme.title: Rear Window (1954)
programme.desc: A photographer is left housebound and in a wheelchair after breaking his leg in an accident. He passes the time watching the comings and goings of his neighbours in the apartment block opposite and becomes convinced one of them has committed a murder. However, he has trouble persuading anyone to believe his story. Hitchcock thriller, starring James Stewart, Grace Kelly, Raymond Burr, Thelma Ritter and Judith Evelyn

programme.title: The Championship
programme.desc: Armagh v Tyrone (Throw-in 4.15pm). Mark Sidebottom presents coverage of the Ulster Senior Football Championship first-round match at BOX-IT Athletic Grounds. These teams met in the semi-finals last year, with Armagh claiming victory but narrowly losing out to Donegal after extra time in the showpiece match. With analysis by Philly McMahon, Conor Meyler and Niall Grimley, pitchside reports from Sarah Mulkerrins, and commentary by Thomas Niblock and Conor McManus

programme.title: The Chronicles of Strangford
programme.desc: Sheep are indoors for the winter, while Kerry and his researchers set about tagging oystercatchers and nest coxes are erected for barn owls

programme.title: Paula McIntyres Hamely Kitchen
programme.desc: Paula shares recipes for cullen skink soup with a hunk of cheese and scallion soda bread on the side, and a delicious baked apple pudding. She also heads to Cushendun Beach, where she gives two young fiddle players tips on how to create BBQ pork shoulder, which will be served up in a soordook (buttermilk) bap with pickled celery and apple mayo

programme.title: Olivier Awards 2026
programme.desc: Actor and comedian Nick Mohammed hosts the biggest night in theatre with a star-studded line up from the Royal Albert Hall. Tonight marks the Olivier Awards 50th anniversary and highlights include performances from nominated musicals, Paddington the Musical, Evita, Into the Woods, The Unlikely Pilgrimage of Harold Fry, Shucked and The Producers. Elaine Paige is honoured with this years Special Award and contenders for other big awards include Cate Blanchett, Tom Hiddleston, Marianne Jean-Baptiste, Bryan Cranston, Giles Terera and Rachel Zegler

programme.title: Hell for Leather: The Story of Gaelic Football
programme.desc: Documentary delving into the role of Gaelic football in the sporting, cultural and social history of modern Ireland. Modern masters explore its appeal, with Brian Fenton pouring out his passion for a game that conquered his home city of Dublin, and Tyrones Seán Cavanagh revealing the depth of emotions he felt when he won the All-Ireland and brought home the Sam Maguire cup for the first time

programme.title: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly (1966)
programme.desc: A laconic gunslinger, a ruthless hired killer and a conniving outlaw hunt for a cache of loot during the chaos of the American Civil War. However, each is privy to only part of the information necessary to find it, setting in motion a series of deadly betrayals. The third film in Sergio Leones Spaghetti Western trilogy, starring Clint Eastwood as the Man with No Name, with Lee Van Cleef and Eli Wallach. Featuring Ennio Morricones iconic soundtrack

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: The comedian visits Uganda to find out whether Brits should add this country to their holiday bucket list. He is hosted by Bugandan princess Alex, who teaches him about Ugandas history before and after dictator Idi Amins rule

programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: Flashbacks show Stephen and Lisas first meeting, while Amandas pregnancy and Daves ambitions create uncertainty for all

programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: At a friends wedding, Lisa struggles in the face of Amandas pregnancy, while Stephen tries to find the courage to confront Dave. Drama starring Paapa Essiedu and Siobhán Cullen as a couple dealing with the aftermath of pregnancy loss, with Charlotte Riley and Jack Bannon

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents a compilation edition focusing on motoring memorabilia, with items including a vintage stop sign that brought an Oswestry team to an abrupt halt and a duo in Essex who used magic to drive down the price of a luxury car mascot. Eric journeys along the Time Road exhibition at the British Motor Museum and in the Cotswolds, Christina Trevanion goes for a spin at one of Britains most historic racing circuits

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the hidden wild world of Britains backyards. The first episode focuses on the garden of a thousand-year-old mill house in Oxfordshire. As the winter floods recede, a kingfisher searches for a mate, a vole must evade a range of dangers, and otters and red kites patrol the river, both day and night

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Chelsea v Manchester City at Stamford Bridge and Nottingham Forest v Aston Villa at The City Ground. City were unbeaten in their last 12 matches with Chelsea in all competitions, but the sides drew 1-1 in the reverse fixture at Etihad Stadium in January. Forest and the Villans were both in needs of points for differing reasons, with the hosts battling to avoid relegation and Unai Emerys men aiming to qualify for the Champions League. Plus, Crystal Palace v Newcastle United and Sunderland v Tottenham Hotspur

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight players do battle in the knockout quiz. Only one will be left standing at the end, hoping to reach the Golden Run and double their money. Hosted by Alex Scott

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Clive Myries Caribbean Adventure
programme.desc: In Jamaicas capital city, Clive discovers a new hidden talent when he tries out some dancehall moves. He explores a 19th-century mansion, which tells the story of Jamaicas first black millionaire. Clive heads to the school that has produced most of the countrys Olympic sprinting talent and samples a Jamaican-Irish stout punch

programme.title: Natures Epic Journeys
programme.desc: Documentary following 100,000 caribou as they face bears, wolves, frozen rivers and rugged mountains to undertake the worlds longest land migration. Liz Bonnin and a team of scientists track the herd as they travel 5,000km through the Arctic wilderness of Canada and Alaska, revealing insights into the lives of individual animals as they attempt the epic journey. Cameras capture the caribou as they fight to reach their calving grounds before they give birth - failure would spell disaster

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The experts gather in Hartlepool, where they evaluate a Steiff teddy bear bought for a dog to play with, and a large Chinese paper passport that helped to save lives. Other items include a piece of shrapnel from the 1914 bombardment of the town and a 17th-century jar with possible ties to Oliver Cromwell

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Thomas Plant and Adam Partridge look out for potential treasures at Chiddingstone Castle in Kent, while presenter Paul Martin visits Chartwell House, home to Winston Churchill, to uncover the real man behind the political persona

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph, Ben Thompson, Laura Tobin and Henning Wehn test their general knowledge skills in a variety of fun trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads to Japan for a trip through the country, beginning his first leg in its second city Osaka, which is famous for its food culture, nightlife and friendly people. He then takes a ride on a Shinkansen bullet train and end in Nagoya, Japans manufacturing hub

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: Investigations from BBC correspondents all over the world, revealing secrets, rooting out injustice and exposing crime, corruption and abuse

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.desc: The comedian matches wildlife sanctuary owner Lindsay with celebrity artist and animal lover Ricky Wilson, but the Kaiser Chiefs frontman faces an artistic dilemma. Lindsay wants the portrait to focus on the animals she has dedicated her life to, but Ricky believes it is Lindsay who is extraordinary and should be at the centre of the painting

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie hosts the concluding semi-final of the quiz, with four returning contenders answering questions on the specialist subjects of Amelia Earhart, Laurence Olivier, the red wines of Burgundy, and the songs of Noël Coward. They are then each tested on general knowledge as they try to win the remaining place in the grand final

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Manchester take on Imperial College London in the concluding semi-final, hoping to take one step closer to lifting the series trophy. Amol Rajan asks the questions

programme.title: Miriam Margolyes Made Me Me
programme.desc: Director Simon Draper set out to make a podcast with actress Miriam Margolyes, but the project soon became sidetracked as he was drawn ever deeper into her orbit. The podcast turned into a chaotic, joyful road trip, revealing Miriams true legacy isnt just her work, but the lives she has quietly transformed, beginning with the film-maker himself

programme.title: Éadaí SOS
programme.desc: Caoimhe Ní Chathail and Proinsias Ó Coinn return to challenge more fashionistas to alter their shopping habits, supporting them in reducing, reusing and recycling their clothes. in the opening edition, Caoimhe visits Clodagh, who loves to shop, especially for handbags. Can she be inspired to wear the clothes she already owns and find out more about sustainable handbag options?

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: nsights and global interviews on the news stories of the day, presented by Victoria Derbyshire

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Charmain and the Prophet
programme.desc: Reporter Myles Bonnar investigates the tragedy that befell Arbroath single mum Charmain Speirs after she married Ghanaian preacher Eric Adusah after a whirlwind romance. Less than six months later, Charmain was found dead in a hotel in Ghana. Meeting Charmains family and friends, and those close to the case, Myles asks what happened in Room 112 and investigates the truth behind Adusah, known as the Prophet

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer head to Norfolk on the hunt for tench at Rocklands Mere, a hidden gem in the quaint village of Rocklands St Peter. After a pretty perfect first day at the lakes, they decide to reward themselves with a pint in the Brisley Bell pub, where Bob reveals they are staying overnight, much to Pauls delight. Later, Dr Anand Patel pays them a visit by the lake

programme.title: The Chronicles of Strangford
programme.desc: It is Easter and in Portaferry the plants on the tea plantation are taken out of the poly tunnel. Colin Morgan narrates the documentary

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: Its week 11, and the final five candidates face a gruelling round of interviews with some of Lord Sugars toughest-talking business associates. As their business plans are picked apart, some contestants hold their own, while others crumble under the pressure. In the boardroom, Alan Sugar has a tough decision to make, as only two can make it through to next weeks grand final

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.sub-title: Easter: The Sights and Sounds of Spring
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor visit Bisterne Estate in Hampshires Avon Valley on Easter Sunday, where farmland and water meadows provide a vital home for lapwings and hares. Plus, author Robert Macfarlane shares an exclusive spring reading, and on his Cotswolds farm, Adam Henson restores a traditional hedgerow, just in time for nesting birds

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey and the team head to Twickenham to help the Allan family tackle their overflowing home. Mum Sharon has ADHD and thrives when things have a place - but standards have slipped and the house has never had a proper clear-out. Daughter Holly, who is autistic, finds comfort in routine and familiar objects and feels overwhelmed by the clutter. As the team snoop around, they soon discover part of the problem - dad Brians secret gadget-buying habit

programme.title: Twenty Twenty Six
programme.desc: Ian Fletcher starts a new chapter in Miami as director of integrity for the Fifa World Cup, overseeing the biggest football event in the world. His first challenge is to lead the Oversight Team towards a decision on which cities should host the semi-final matches. Spoof documentary sequel, starring Hugh Bonneville

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Shift the Thrift

In Hastings, partners James and Elaine scour 15 charity shops, hunting for vintage gems and a hidden star buy before facing a tense, live online auction for charity

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Inferno in the Fields and the Fight to Save Badgers
programme.desc: Police close in on an organised crime gang targeting farms and stealing high-tech GPS systems worth hundreds of thousands of pounds at farms across the north of England. In Leicestershire, a huge barn fire devastates on a family run dairy farm, threatening cattle and destroying vital winter feed. As the flames spread, farmer Blaise and daughter Millie race to save their livestock. In Scotland, reporter Paul Colgan meets farmer Debbie Duffus, as she is reunited with the air ambulance team members who saved her life after a brutal farm accident

programme.desc: When the team deploys a cutting-edge new technology on Skinwalker Ranch to study the mysterious Bubble, it leads to an unsettling revelation

programme.title: Titanic: A Dead Reckoning
programme.desc: As the Titanic started to sink in April 1912, another ship came into view, offering her passengers the hope of being rescued. However, despite a fusillade of rockets, the ship turned away. Within two hours, 1,500 people were dead. An American investigation at the time accused the SS Californian, even though the freighter was stationary that night. A British inquiry concurred, keen to shift blame from national failures on lifeboat provision and steamship supervision. However, since Titanics wreck was discovered, the mystery began to unlock, and now, with recently discovered clues, a new suspect has emerged: the SS Mount Temple. This documentary follows a range of experts, using the latest investigative tools, as they examine these new clues and revisit the evidence from 1912 to take a fresh look at the cold case of the 20th century

programme.title: Secrets of the Lost Liners
programme.desc: Documentary charting the fate of the MTS Oceanos, a French-built and Greek-owned cruise ship that sank off the coast of South Africa in 1991 after suffering uncontrolled flooding to its engine room

programme.title: Historys Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: The theories behind where the Ark of the Covenant - an artefact thought to be the most sacred relic of the Israelites - may be hidden

programme.title: Egypts Unexplained Files
programme.desc: An exploration of crime and punishment in ancient Egypt. The topic features homicidal royalty, rampant tomb raiding and acts of extreme punishment

programme.sub-title: Orb and Crash Debris, KGB UFOs and Aliens and Superhuman Reflexes
programme.desc: The notion that a silver orb spotted in the sky and alleged crash debris may be from a UFO. Plus, a 65-year-old-man with the fastest hands in the West and an infamous spy organisations alleged footage of UFOs and aliens

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Beastly Howls, Hollowman Air Force Base UFO, and the Cabin That Defies Gravity
programme.desc: A photograph of a UFO hovering over Holloman Air Force Base in New Mexico and a cabin built in a supposedly anomalous spot that defies gravity in the Redwood Forests. Plus, the origin of frightening howls recorded in Witset, British Columbia

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Worm in the Sky, Skeletal Mystery Remains and Thessaloniki Mystery Sounds
programme.desc: A remotely operated vehicle (ROV) finds the remains of a massive skeleton 825 metres below the surface of the Mediterranean. Plus, a building that appears to shrink as a family gets closer to it and a strange worm-shaped object spotted in the sky

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts is in Glasgow to investigate the impact of steam power on Britains major towns in the 18th and 19th centuries. She also learns about the arrival of migrant workers in the city and how the insanitary conditions in which they lived, which made Glasgow both a hotbed of disease and a place of great medical advancement. Aerial archaeologist Ben Robinson flies his drone over Loch Katrine as he tells the story of how Victorian engineers overcame geographical barriers to bring fresh drinking water to the workers of Glasgow

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores Edinburgh and its role in the union between England and Scotland. She discovers how the famous case of Burke and Hares bodysnatching influenced British medical law, and visits the Royal Bank of Scotland, where she uncovers evidence of the worlds first overdraft. Plus, the role played by Henry Dundas, whose statue stands in St Andrews Square, in the continuation of the slave trade

programme.desc: Another team takes on ingenious games and fiendish challenges. Hosted by Richard OBrien

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.title: The Bidding Room
