programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The explorer travels from Texas to southern Mexico, meeting a woman who risks her life to take supplies to migrants stranded on the Mexican side of the border in Reynosa, one of the countrys most violent cities, which is dominated by a powerful criminal organisation. He also ventures into the rainforest to explore the Maya city of Yaxchilan, and meets the modern-day descendants of this civilisation, who are increasingly marginalised and treated as second-class citizens

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels across Central America, from the Caribbean to the Pacific. Along the way, he goes diving on a coral reef on the coast of Belize, and witnesses a gun battle between park rangers and intruders in Chiquibul National Park. He also discovers how climate change has left Guatemala devastated by famine and drought, reports on measures to curb gang violence in El Salvador, and learns how Costa Rica aims to become the worlds first carbon neutral country by 2021

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The racing fan sets out to break the record for the worlds fastest gravity-powered sled, taking expert advice from Winter Olympic gold medallist Amy Williams. Guy experiences his first taste of going blisteringly fast on ice at the famous Cresta Run in the Swiss ski resort of St Moritz, before a crack team of engineers helps him build a prototype toboggan. Then its on to Andorra in the Pyrenees, where he hopes to steer himself into the record books on the new speed ski slope Pista Riberal

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May immerse themselves in nostalgia and try to prove that hot hatches from their younger days are better than their modern equivalents. Jeremy grabs the keys to a Volkswagen Golf GTI, Richard takes the wheel of a Vauxhall Nova SRi and James May opts for the Ford Fiesta XR2i, and the trio set off on a road trip that includes a hill climb and a visit to a supermarket. However, their adventure concludes at an army training ground in Wales, where they and their steeds must face the heavily armed Top Gear Police Department. Downton Abbey star Hugh Bonneville blasts the Reasonably Priced Car around the test track

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond gets behind the wheel of the mid-engined Alfa Romeo 4C on the shores of Lake Como in Italy - but his enjoyment of the lightweight vehicle is disturbed by the arrival of Jeremy Clarkson, who demands a race against his quad bike. Jeremy also visits Belgium, putting the McLaren P1 hypercar to the test in Bruges and around the Spa-Francorchamps Formula One circuit - and its combined power output of more than 900bhp generated from electricity and turbocharged petrol V8 power is likely to cause astonishment. Meanwhile, James May spends time at Camp Bastion in Afghanistan to learn about the armys vehicle redeployment efforts, and actor Tom Hiddleston tries to coax the best time he can muster from the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.desc: Laura and David spend the night together, and Charles refuses to attend his fathers funeral

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The elderly sleuth makes a determined effort to drum up more business, and is hired to find a womans missing son. She faces a race against time to track down the troubled youngster before he decides theres no point in going on. Detective drama, starring Patricia Routledge

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon tries to make the best of her job at the bakery and Dorien joins the Open University, while Tracey continues to study at the school of hard knocks. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy hatches a plan to erect a flag in a prominent position - but first he needs to find one, and a pole to put it on. Brian Wilde stars

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggys dream concerning his flag finally comes to fruition, while Compo spots a romantic opportunity. Brian Wilde and Bill Owen star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Deep in the Heart of Yorkshire
programme.desc: When Sid and Wally are seen disappearing into the woods, their wives Ivy and Nora wonder what they are up to, so press Compo, Clegg and Foggy to find out. Long-running comedy set in Yorkshire, written by Roy Clarke

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: As the Brokenwood Golf Club gathers for its Wednesday morning round, a zombie-like figure lurches from the morning mist identified as Alison Stone, the wealthy club owner. Her face red and blistering, she collapses into a bunker and dies leaving Mike and Kristin to investigate an apparent homicide by poisoning. Detective drama, starring Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland, Pana Hema Taylor and Nic Sampson

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Brian struggles to control his urge to drink and finds refuge in a commune at the centre of the teams investigation into the death of a university student. The case also helps Gerry to face up to his relationship with Emily - who has not spoken to him since she discovered the truth about the DNA test at Ricky Hansons trial. Police drama, starring Alun Armstrong and Amanda Redman

programme.title: Dalziel and Pascoe
programme.desc: The coppers investigate the discovery of a body unearthed during renovations at a new university, and are left puzzling over blackmail, mistaken identity and possible suicide. Feature-length detective drama, with Warren Clarke, Colin Buchanan and Prunella Scales

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The elderly sleuth makes a determined effort to drum up more business, and is hired to find a womans missing son. She faces a race against time to track down the troubled youngster before he decides theres no point in going on. Detective drama, starring Patricia Routledge

programme.title: Whitstable Pearl
programme.desc: A young woman receives an anonymous note claiming that her husband did not take his own life seven years ago and the widow hires Pearl to investigate

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: Sister Boniface is called to examine the body of Hilary Sympson-Smythe, who has died mysteriously, her face covered in cold cream and a completed jigsaw with one piece missing. A lonely hearts connection to Ruth Pennys newspaper leads Gillespie and Livingstone to investigate the male respondents. Ruth believes there is another victim who died in identical circumstances

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Beech reopens old wounds by trying to pin corruption charges on one of Dalys friends, and calls in a favour from Rickman

programme.title: Doctors
programme.sub-title: Youll Be a Man My Son
programme.desc: Archie helps a patient with Alzheimers disease understand what happened to his son, who was born during wartime and had to be given up for adoption. Meanwhile, Michelle frogmarches a boy out of the treatment room because of his inappropriate behaviour, but Julie delves deeper to find a hidden motive. Matt Kennard and Donnaleigh Bailey star

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Julia comes to the aid of a couple suffering a downturn in fortune, while Melody, feeling she has been unfairly treated by Nick, requests that George review her case. Vivien admits she has not been herself recently when she snaps at fellow receptionist Ruth. Diane Keen and Elizabeth Bower star

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: On the day of the christening, Chrissie has a shocking announcement for Owen. Will faces a tough decision as his work and family priorities clash and Jess feels overjoyed about her relationship with Zubin

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Kirsty faces unwelcome inquiries from the police, who are investigating Warrens accident, and Nitas behaviour gives her even more cause for concern after the youngster witnesses her mother being comforted by Adam. Meanwhile, Jays concentration is affected by the discovery of a testicular lump, and when he finally confesses his fears to Miriam, she persuades him to see a doctor. Medical drama, starring Lucy Gaskell, Tristan Gemmill and Ben Turner

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough swims alongside sea otters, discovering he is no match for their natural agility in the water, while in Florida he takes a dip with manatees for the first time and observes their feeding habits. On the coast of California, he encounters the largest mammals on the planet, blue whales, and reveals how they, along with porpoises and dolphins, learned to court, mate and give birth in the water

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob takes Terry on a tour of their old haunts - only to reveal most of them have been demolished. Terry finds it difficult accepting that times have changed during his time away - and a job interview proves the last straw. Classic comedy, with James Bolam and Rodney Bewes

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: First episode of the classic comedy sequel. Bob travels down to London in his efforts to find a suitable wedding present, where fate sees him reunited with Terry, returning home after a gruelling spell in the Army. Starring Rodney Bewes and James Bolam. From 1973

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Terry arrives home to find his parents on holiday, the fridge empty, the house cold and Thelma her usual unhelpful self. Luckily Bob is on hand with a suggestion. Comedy, starring James Bolam and Rodney Bewes, with Brigit Forsyth

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob has doubts about his marriage when he realises all his peers - even the vicar - seem to be getting divorced. Classic comedy, starring Rodney Bewes and James Bolam

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob takes Terry on a tour of their old haunts - only to reveal most of them have been demolished. Terry finds it difficult accepting that times have changed during his time away - and a job interview proves the last straw. Classic comedy, with James Bolam and Rodney Bewes

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring a bizarre block in Taiwan resembling a giant speaker, a pioneering venture in Alaska, and an alpine village that masks a military beast

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the wild and rugged coastline of Northumberland by rail, beginning in Morpeth, where he finds an historic collection of Northumbrian bagpipes and discovers how difficult they are to play. The East Coast mainline delivers Michael to Widdrington and its long sandy bay, where a beachcombing blacksmith finds treasures to inspire artwork in steel

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Farne Islands to Berwick upon Tweed
programme.desc: Michael Portillos rail trek along the coast of Northumberland continues towards the Farne Islands, takes in the holy island of Lindisfarne, and finishes in Englands northernmost town, Berwick-upon-Tweed

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a trip from the Firth of Clyde to Lewis and Harris in the Outer Hebrides, beginning with a visit to a nuclear power station on the Hunterston Peninsula. He then learns about Largs Viking past and admires one of the most beautiful railway stations in Britain at Wemyss Bay. The importance of ferries to connect western Scotland with its many islands becomes plain to Michael at Port Glasgow in the deep waters of the Clyde, where he visits Ferguson Marine, builders of Calmac ferries

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Onboard the West Highland Line, Michael Portillo explores the stunning scenery of Scotlands west coast and islands as he heads to the Hebridean archipelago from the ferry terminal of Oban. On his way to the Isle of Mull, Michael watches eagerly for sea mammals in a stretch of water where many species of whales, dolphins and porpoises have been recorded, before checking out the daily catch in the fishing port of Tobermory. Later, Michael heads to the tiny Isle of Staffa, where he follows in the wake of a young Felix Mendelssohn, who was only 20-year-old when he set eyes on the volcanic sea cavern, Fingals Cave

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.sub-title: Londons Lost Route to the Sea
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales go in search of the lost route between London and the sea, which has not been fully open for more than 200 years and requires four different boats to traverse now. They leave the Thames at Teddington and travel through the Surrey and Sussex countryside before arriving at Guildford, and a canal-side theatre where they have both regularly performed. The final leg of the trip brings them to the River Arun before reaching the English Channel

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales travel from Birmingham to Braunston, Northamptonshire, to attend a festival of vintage canal boats, aided on the tricky journey by their son Sam and Juliet, Timothys daughter from his first marriage. Along the way, they meet a 78-year-old boatman, who reveals how he was born on a canal boat, and unveil a plaque dedicated to a woman who dedicated her life to preserving Britains canals

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales go to Ireland and travel along the Shannon Erne Waterway, a canal linking Northern Ireland with the Republic of Ireland. Along the way, they try some traditional dancing and folk singing, visit historic sites including Crom Castle and Bloody Pass, and meet a genealogist with a fascinating story to tell

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales set forth from Gothenburg on the first half of the Gota Canal, one of the longest and most beautiful waterways in the world

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the hunting tactics of the worlds carnivores. Arctic foxes in the frozen north must collect food during warmer times to survive the winter, while the big cats of the southern hemisphere rely on stealth and surprise to catch each meal. The presenter also reveals how leopards and tigers are solitary hunters, coming together only to mate, whereas lions stay in family groups so they can tackle larger prey and better protect their young

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the world of omnivores, natures generalists, revealing their myriad talents in the wild and how some species have adapted to mans encroachment into their habitats by making their homes in cities and towns

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough swims alongside sea otters, discovering he is no match for their natural agility in the water, while in Florida he takes a dip with manatees for the first time and observes their feeding habits. On the coast of California, he encounters the largest mammals on the planet, blue whales, and reveals how they, along with porpoises and dolphins, learned to court, mate and give birth in the water

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough profiles the tree-dwellers of the animal kingdom, as primates, bats and squirrels perform complicated manoeuvres in the worlds forests. Whether aided by sharp claws, webbed feet or gripping tails, these animals play a complicated game of survival with predators lurking below, ready to make a meal of those who fail to stay aloft

programme.title: American Historys Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian reveals the myths and deceptions told following the United States emergence as a superpower after the Second World War. The 1950s and early 1960s in America is remembered as a golden era of abundance, harmony and the American dream made real. Lucy Worsley reveals that to have been a carefully constructed illusion and that the era of Americas supremacy was a time of government deception, racial conflict and fears of nuclear annihilation

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson discovers how the Cornish village of Botallack was the centre of the Cornish tin and copper mining industry and supplied a deadly poison to the world. On the coast of California, he encounters the largest mammals on the planet, blue whales, and reveals how they, along with porpoises and dolphins, learned to court, mate and give birth in the water

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough profiles the tree-dwellers of the animal kingdom, as primates, bats and squirrels perform complicated manoeuvres in the worlds forests. Whether aided by sharp claws, webbed feet or gripping tails, these animals play a complicated game of survival with predators lurking below, ready to make a meal of those who fail to stay aloft

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the hunting tactics of the worlds carnivores. Arctic foxes in the frozen north must collect food during warmer times to survive the winter, while the big cats of the southern hemisphere rely on stealth and surprise to catch each meal. The presenter also reveals how leopards and tigers are solitary hunters, coming together only to mate, whereas lions stay in family groups so they can tackle larger prey and better protect their young

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the world of omnivores, natures generalists, revealing their myriad talents in the wild and how some species have adapted to mans encroachment into their habitats by making their homes in cities and towns

programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: The historian investigates the complex factors that shaped the dark imagination of a refined Devonshire lady, discovering family secrets and a childhood haunted by a sinister figure. Focusing on the first third of Agatha Christies life, this episode unearths the surprising roots of her most compelling themes, the inspiration for some of her greatest creations, and the secrets that the enigmatic Christie herself kept carefully hidden from public view

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson discovers how the Cornish village of Botallack was the centre of the Cornish tin and copper mining industry and supplied a deadly poison to the world. Local resident Paula Nicholls explains how her ancestors, the Boyne family, owned a mine which was involved in a catastrophic explosion in 1893 where 20 men tragically died

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Raquel invites her estranged parents around for dinner, and Del-Boy is keen to make an impression - but a mix-up with the gravy boat puts a damper on proceedings. However, things soon take a turn for the better when Dels father-in-law finds an old watch among the Trotters junk, which he claims could net them a pretty packet at auction. But nothing can quite prepare them for the shock they have in store. The final part of the Christmas 1996 trilogy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses

programme.desc: A retired jeweller offers Del-Boy the deal of a lifetime - then suddenly has a heart attack and gets taken to hospital with a briefcase containing 250 cut-price gold necklaces still chained to his wrist. Comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del enters one of Rodneys old paintings in a competition and is over the moon when he wins a holiday for three at a five-star hotel in Majorca. programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Dels beloved mum apparently makes contact during a seance at the Nags Head, inspiring him to visit the doctor to seek treatment for stomach cramps. Comedy, with David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Rodney ties the knot with Cassandra, but Del has more on his mind than best man duties as the dreaded Driscoll brothers arrive at the Nags Head baying for his blood over some stolen mobile phones. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Gwyneth Strong

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del feels down on his luck after the council rejects his application for a home improvement grant, but his spirits are raised when he hears about a fancy-dress party across town. With Tessa Peake-Jones, Gwyneth Strong, Douglas Hodge and Angela Bruce

programme.desc: Del buys a new manual to help him show Raquel more appreciation and give him the confidence to make positive decisions. The second part of the Christmas 1996 trilogy, starring David Jason, Tessa Peake-Jones and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.desc: A retired jeweller offers Del-Boy the deal of a lifetime - then suddenly has a heart attack and gets taken to hospital with a briefcase containing 250 cut-price gold necklaces still chained to his wrist. Comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del enters one of Rodneys old paintings in a competition and is over the moon when he wins a holiday for three at a five-star hotel in Majorca. programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Dels beloved mum apparently makes contact during a seance at the Nags Head, inspiring him to visit the doctor to seek treatment for stomach cramps. Comedy, with David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Rodney ties the knot with Cassandra, but Del has more on his mind than best man duties as the dreaded Driscoll brothers arrive at the Nags Head baying for his blood over some stolen mobile phones. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Gwyneth Strong

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del feels down on his luck after the council rejects his application for a home improvement grant, but his spirits are raised when he hears about a fancy-dress party across town. With Tessa Peake-Jones, Gwyneth Strong, Douglas Hodge and Angela Bruce

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del buys a new manual to help him show Raquel more appreciation and give him the confidence to make positive decisions. The second part of the Christmas 1996 trilogy, starring David Jason, Tessa Peake-Jones and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Raquel invites her estranged parents around for dinner, and Del-Boy is keen to make an impression - but a mix-up with the gravy boat puts a damper on proceedings. programme.desc: Ben is disappointed by the inheritance he receives when his uncle dies, while another investment causes problems for the dentist. Robert Lindsay stars

programme.title: My Family

programme.desc: Susan wants a dog, but the assessor from the dogs home deems her unsuitable, so Ben goes to the kennels to sneak one out for her, only to find she is already there and about to call in the press as a protest against the assessors decision

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats a seven-month-old fox red Labrador puppy with lameness, caused by a joint defect called osteochondritis dissecans. He explains that medicine can be given, but in the end the dog may end up requiring a complete ankle replacement or a joint fusion on both sides. Jo has travelled 400 miles to bring her beloved four-year-old springer spaniel to see Noel. The poochs kneecap keeps slipping in and out of her knee joint, and despite several previous procedures, the problems persist. After an examination by Noel, Jo faces the further worry of osteoarthritis

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Spaniel Scrumpy is suffering from lameness and Noel discovers he has a collapsed disc and severe elbow arthritis. He already has a chequered medical history totalling six previous operations to his elbows, hip and knee. Meanwhile, there is an emergency admission when a 15-month-old dachshund arrives with a mystery brain condition, and Noel and his neurology colleagues have their work cut out to identify the cause. When his condition rapidly deteriorates overnight, suddenly the life of this much-loved young dog hangs in the balance

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Four-year-old pug and beagle cross Barney is brought into Fitzpatrick Referrals after being hit by a bus. The dog has lost 40 per cent of the bone in the damaged leg, so Noel creates an innovative two-tier treatment, involving an external ankle frame for support. Sisters Rosemary and Diane bring in their five-year-old basset hound Albert when they find he is suddenly unable to move his back legs, and 10-month-old Labrador cross Gylly receives treatment for lameness and an unusual bone deformity

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Golden retriever poodle-cross Sukis front legs have developed abnormally and now require urgent attention as the bones of her forearms are growing at different rates, so Noel decides that major surgery is required. Beloved elderly Labrador Raffa is nearly 11 and has become lame with constant pain in his front legs, and Noel discovers cartilage erosion in both elbows. Meanwhile, five-year-old dachshund Zizi is rushed in after suddenly losing the use of her back legs overnight, and an urgent MRI scan confirms that Zizi has suffered an exploded disc

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A couple bring their 13-month-old cocker spaniel puppy in for scheduled surgery for hip dysplasia which will require two separate full hip replacements. Elsewhere, two-year-old cat Jazz is rushed in by mum Sarah suffering from a smashed femur and shattered thorax after being hit by a car, and eight-year-old dachshund Olive is treated after suddenly losing the use of her back legs

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics in Oxford treat a woman suffering from a mystery illness, while in Portsmouth, the crews treat an elderly diabetic patient with dangerously low blood sugar

The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the DIY SOS team transform the home of a police officer who was a victim of the Westminster Bridge terrorist attack in March 2017, suffering horrific injuries which left him wheelchair bound. Months after the attack took place, Kris still resides in hospital as his north London home is inaccessible for him, so the team and hundreds of volunteers work to get Kris living back at home to his family

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and team, together with West Bromwich volunteers, helps a grieving grandmother and her family who are making do in a small house. Sandras daughter Crystal Chambers passed away in 2015, so she and Crystals 19-year-old brother Ziggy are doing their best to raise youngsters Deago and Ameira amid cramped conditions

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Part one of two. Nick Knowles and the team undertake their biggest and longest build yet, constructing two buildings for the Grenfell community over several months to create spaces worth around £2million. With the aid of large construction companies, generous suppliers, volunteers and the Duke of Cambridge - who visits the site - the team starts by building a new home for the Dale Youth Boxing Club. The club started the careers of James DeGale, who won an Olympic gold medal in Beijing 10 years ago, and George Groves, current WBA Super Middleweight champion, as well as producing many national champions

programme.title: Tipping Point

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats chocolate Labrador Inca, who has a history of developmental elbow disease and osteoarthritis that has led to her elbow joints becoming worn out. Two-year-old Cavapoo Nala, who has hip dysplasia, and Great Dane Charles, who is unsteady on his feet due to a squashed spinal cord, are also on the patients list. Charles owner Steve knows only too well that spinal surgery is a hugely serious undertaking, as he himself had multiple surgeries following a bike accident a decade earlier

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick fears the worst when a young cockapoo is hit by a lawnmower, and he faces the challenge of saving as much of its leg as possible. Meanwhile, a seven-year-old labrador struggles with pain in its elbows, and a German Shepherd-Collie cross is suffering with a bulging disk in his spin and hip dysplasia

programme.title: DIY SOS

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick tends to a Newfoundland called Billy, who clocks up thousands of miles travelling back and forth from his home in Aberdeen for treatment on its legs. He also attempts to help Barney, a Yorkshire terrier that has a deformity in its neck that is compressing its spinal cord. For owners Linda and Barry, Noel is their last chance, but the operation involves high-risk surgery with no guarantee of success

programme.title: Gaia: A Death on Dancing Ledge
programme.desc: Zara McDermott revisits the case of 19-year-old Gaia Pope-Sutherland, highlighting her familys search for truth and justice in the wake of her untimely death from hypothermia. Gaias alleged attacker contacted her 10 months before she went missing. As her attempts to get a restraining order were declined, her state of mind deteriorated, requiring her to be sectioned under the Mental Health Act. The inquest into Gaias death - one of the longest in British history - revealed vital clues into her final weeks, unearthing over 50 missed opportunities in her care and search for her

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis meets the Canadian comedian, actor and writer, Katherine Ryan, for a frank and honest conversation about her career, relationships and the changing shape of her family. Louis accompanies Katherine as she performs her latest stand-up tour, Missus, in Southend-On-Sea, and later, he spends a rare day off with Katherine, her daughter Violet and her husband Bobby

programme.title: The Mormons Are Coming
programme.desc: Documentary following three young missionaries as they go through an intensive two-week induction at the Mormon training centre in Chorley, Lancashire. Giving up their first names to become known as Elders and Sisters, they have limited contact with family as they live under a strict timetable including role play and lessons on how to come across well to non-believers

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: South Central paramedics race to a newborn with a low temperature, while crews in Oxford and Portsmouth are kept busy with a man vomiting blood and a patients hip pain

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis spends time with Dominic Harrison - aka singer and musician Yungblud - as he joins him during his whirlwind tour. Along the way, Louis meets fans who follow Yungblud with almost religious intensity, before sitting down with the singer to discuss a range of topics including his mental health, his sexuality and his response to outspoken critics

programme.title: Dangerous Breed: Crime. Cons. Cats.
programme.desc: With Samanthas family desperate for news, Teddy answers Fredericks questions. As new information comes to light, one of Teddys ex-students speaks up

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: A handsome stranger comes to the rescue when Susan is stranded at an Albanian airport, and the dashing American invites her to join him on his round-the-world adventure. Ben is surprised when a celebrity dentist dedicates a new book to him, and finds out the author is an old friend from college who is actually jealous of the family mans mundane lifestyle. Guest starring John Barrowman and David Haig

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben goes undercover in a hotel when he suspects Susan of having an affair with a wealthy client, but finds Janey there instead. After recovering from the shock, he begins imagining the benefits of a rich son-in-law - until Susan sees him kissing another woman. Guest starring Robert Bathurst

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Susan offers her support to an old friend going through a divorce, and Ben is roped in as a date to make the womans husband jealous. However, a large amount of celebratory wine leads to a tricky situation. Elsewhere, Janey invents a Swedish woman to be Rogers love interest. Guest starring Belinda Lang (2point4 Children)

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben is disappointed by the inheritance he receives when his uncle dies, while another investment causes problems for the dentist. Robert Lindsay stars

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: When Ben and Susan discover Michael has been seeing a therapist, but will not tell his parents why, they are prompted to undertake a spot of self-analysis

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Susan wants a dog, but the assessor from the dogs home deems her unsuitable, so Ben goes to the kennels to sneak one out for her, only to find she is already there and about to call in the press as a protest against the assessors decision

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. programme.title: Tipping Point

The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats a seven-month-old fox red Labrador puppy with lameness, caused by a joint defect called osteochondritis dissecans. He explains that medicine can be given, but in the end the dog may end up requiring a complete ankle replacement or a joint fusion on both sides. Jo has travelled 400 miles to bring her beloved four-year-old springer spaniel to see Noel. The poochs kneecap keeps slipping in and out of her knee joint, and despite several previous procedures, the problems persist. After an examination by Noel, Jo faces the further worry of osteoarthritis

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Spaniel Scrumpy is suffering from lameness and Noel discovers he has a collapsed disc and severe elbow arthritis. He already has a chequered medical history totalling six previous operations to his elbows, hip and knee. Meanwhile, there is an emergency admission when a 15-month-old dachshund arrives with a mystery brain condition, and Noel and his neurology colleagues have their work cut out to identify the cause. When his condition rapidly deteriorates overnight, suddenly the life of this much-loved young dog hangs in the balance

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Four-year-old pug and beagle cross Barney is brought into Fitzpatrick Referrals after being hit by a bus. The dog has lost 40 per cent of the bone in the damaged leg, so Noel creates an innovative two-tier treatment, involving an external ankle frame for support. Sisters Rosemary and Diane bring in their five-year-old basset hound Albert when they find he is suddenly unable to move his back legs, and 10-month-old Labrador cross Gylly receives treatment for lameness and an unusual bone deformity

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Golden retriever poodle-cross Sukis front legs have developed abnormally and now require urgent attention as the bones of her forearms are growing at different rates, so Noel decides that major surgery is required. Beloved elderly Labrador Raffa is nearly 11 and has become lame with constant pain in his front legs, and Noel discovers cartilage erosion in both elbows. Meanwhile, five-year-old dachshund Zizi is rushed in after suddenly losing the use of her back legs overnight, and an urgent MRI scan confirms that Zizi has suffered an exploded disc

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A couple bring their 13-month-old cocker spaniel puppy in for scheduled surgery for hip dysplasia which will require two separate full hip replacements. Elsewhere, two-year-old cat Jazz is rushed in by mum Sarah suffering from a smashed femur and shattered thorax after being hit by a car, and eight-year-old dachshund Olive is treated after suddenly losing the use of her back legs

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics in Oxford treat a woman suffering from a mystery illness, while in Portsmouth, the crews treat an elderly diabetic patient with dangerously low blood sugar

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: The crews attend a man who has fallen off his bike and a four-year-old struggling with tonsillitis. Plus, a 35-year-old man with chest pains proves very hard to find

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: The team tackles a dramatic operation where a patients heart is stopped and restarted. Zoe has a stent replaced in her ureter, which was damaged by her treatment for cancer

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Trisha has an investigation of her womb to find out if she has cancer, Steves varicose veins are stripped from his leg, and Dennis needs an operation to strengthen his eyelid

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Comedy sketches, featuring Paul and Sam, the foul-mouthed Nan and Bernie the incompetent nurse

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: More comedy sketches, including Nan visiting a pound shop, and Lauren discovering she has not been invited to a party

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: A death row wife finds love with a convicted murderer, and new parents are determined not to let their baby run their lives. Peter Kay guest stars

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis goes off-grid as he spends time in the company of the adventurer and television presenter Bear Grylls, travelling with him to his private island off the coast of Wales where he is staying for the summer with his wife, Shara. Louis later accompanies Bear on trips to a scout troop in south London and a Marine reserve training camp, before the pair head to Devon where Bear is guest of honour at his own family adventure festival

programme.title: Wagspiracy: Vardy v Rooney
programme.desc: With access to key individuals who didnt give evidence at the trial, this documentary explores the hidden workings of the tabloids, PRs and agents connected to so-called Wagatha Christie, which followed accuisations between footballers wives Coleen Rooney and Rebekah Vardy. Contributors include Colin Paterson and Lizzie Cundy

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team helps project manager Ben in Avening in the Cotswolds, whose life was changed in a split second by a freak accident while holidaying in Cyprus. Ben slipped and fell head first into the shallow end of a pool, breaking his neck in three places, and for him to move back home from Salisbury Spinal Unit, it needs to be adapted with a front access and a two-storey extension with through-floor lift, along with all the bespoke care provision that he will need, including a home-office space

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave Mathewson has put his best clean T-shirt on to collect a 1965 Land Rover from a stately home - and it turns out that the trusty car has a real pedigree

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson picks up a lovingly restored 1925 Calthorpe motorbike that he thinks is more of a work of art than anything ever produced by Picasso

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave Mathewson gets wind of a possible free lunch at the officers mess when he heads off to collect a stylish Swedish Volvo P1800S from an RAF base in the Midlands

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson has sold everything from trucks to bicycles - but he cant remember the last time he auctioned off a two-seater plane

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The latest stage of Michael Portillos trek takes him past lochs and castles on the Far North Line to Britains northernmost railway station at Thurso. From Lairg on the banks of Loch Shin, Michael heads for a secret conservation area in the 18-mile-long loch to track down one of Scotlands most elusive and endangered species, the freshwater pearl mussel

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: From Britains northernmost station at Thurso in Caithness, Michael Portillo heads for the ferry port of Scrabster to cross the Pentland Firth to the Orkney Islands. Passing the Old Man of Hoy, Michael takes in stunning views of the Orkney archipelago and the rugged coastlines of its 70 islands

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off from Tynemouth on the first leg of a trip to Berwick-upon-Tweed, hearing about of a 19th-century tragedy at sea that inspired 140 local men to set up a coastal rescue service. At Long Sands beach, Michael rolls up his sleeves to help volunteers caring for sick and injured seals at the Tynemouth Aquarium Seal Hospital

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads from Newcastle to Lynemouth, and is intrigued to hear of its part in Newcastles early industrial development. En route, Michael travels through the huge Northumberland coalfield and, at Cramlington, he alights to visit an extraordinary work of landform art made from the spoil of a coalmine, the vast Northumberlandia, known as the Lady of the North

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The drop in morale of Churchills forces following the fall of France to Nazi forces during the Second World War, and how the Dunkirk evacuation and the Battle of Britain helped to restore British confidence. Plus, Americas reasons for initially refusing to enter the conflict

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: An account of Hitlers decision to invade the Soviet Union, which produced an initial series of dazzling victories until the harsh winter of 1941 took hold, with fatal consequences for the ill-equipped German army. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates this documentary chronicling events leading up to the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbour on December 7, 1941, and the subsequent collapse of unprepared British forces before the advancing enemy in Singapore. Includes interviews with veterans from both sides

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Factors influencing Americas decision to support Britain during the Second World War, and how Hitlers declaration of war on the US convinced Congress to take more decisive action. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming sets out on the last leg of his journey across central England. While crossing an epic aqueduct near Stratford-upon-Avon, the narrowboat enthusiast gets stuck in a lock in the heart of Birmingham. First broadcast 9 December 2020

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater Robbie Cumming begins a 170-mile journey across the north of England. However, he soon runs into difficulty when he encounters a propeller problem in Wigan and runs aground in Blackburn. Nevertheless, he pushes on to navigate a true wonder of the waterways, the Burnley Embankment

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater and film-maker Robbie Cumming crosses the Pennines on the picturesque Leeds and Liverpool Canal, and lends a hand to hire boaters in Gargrave, North Yorkshire. He also encounters Bingley Five Rise Locks, a wonder of the inland waterways

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater Robbie Cumming explores the Victorian vWest Yorkshire village of Saltaire on the Leeds and Liverpool Canal. He also moors up in Leeds and goes in search of an abandoned waterway on the Aire and Calder Navigation

programme.title: Britains Ancient Capital: Secrets of Orkney
programme.desc: An investigation into how recent archaeological finds on the archipelago indicate that Orkney was the cultural capital of Stone Age Britain and the birthplace of the stone circle cult. Chris Packham uncovers the secrets revealed by the DNA of Orkneys unique vole, Neil Oliver explores tombs and monuments, Shini Somara experiments to discover how ancient Orcadians could have moved giant blocks of stone over rough ground, and Andy Torbet climbs a challenging sea-stack to unlock the story of Orkneys unusual geology

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which the explorer travels through Cornwall as its emerges from the first lockdown, discovering the impact of Covid-19 on a part of the country highly reliant on tourism. Simon discovers the ongoing impact of the collapse of the tin-mining industry in the 1980s when he visits one of Britains poorest estates, and meets the entrepreneurs who sell homemade food on the beach who are struggling to make enough money to last the winter

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the conclusion of his two-part journey, Simon investigates the environmental challenges facing Cornwall and the rest of the country. He discovers how the regions farmers are adopting new, less intensive approaches that are both more profitable and environmentally friendly, and reveals how reintroducing beavers to the uplands could tackle the problem of widespread flooding. He also encounters some of the countys iconic wildlife, including grey seals that live in constant risk of getting caught up in discarded fishing nets

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: On the last leg of his journey across the North and the Midlands, Robbie faces issues with ice on the Grand Union Canal. programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the wild and rugged coastline of Northumberland by rail, beginning in Morpeth, where he finds an historic collection of Northumbrian bagpipes and discovers how difficult they are to play. The East Coast mainline delivers Michael to Widdrington and its long sandy bay, where a beachcombing blacksmith finds treasures to inspire artwork in steel

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels the length of the Northumbrian coastline, with its glorious beaches and grandiose castles. In the village of Alnmouth he takes to the waves in a traditional skiff built by members of the Alnmouth Coastal Rowing Club. His last stop of the day is the mighty Bamburgh Castle

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Farne Islands to Berwick upon Tweed
programme.desc: Michael Portillos rail trek along the coast of Northumberland continues towards the Farne Islands, takes in the holy island of Lindisfarne, and finishes in Englands northernmost town, Berwick-upon-Tweed

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a trip from the Firth of Clyde to Lewis and Harris in the Outer Hebrides, beginning with a visit to a nuclear power station on the Hunterston Peninsula. He then learns about Largs Viking past and admires one of the most beautiful railway stations in Britain at Wemyss Bay. The importance of ferries to connect western Scotland with its many islands becomes plain to Michael at Port Glasgow in the deep waters of the Clyde, where he visits Ferguson Marine, builders of Calmac ferries

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jacks drawn the short straw again as he attempts to shift a couple of 1930s Austin Sevens that have been stuck in the same spot for half a century

programme.title: The World at War

programme.title: True Evil: The Making of a Nazi
programme.desc: Heinrich Himmler, the book-loving loner whose strange mystical beliefs drove him to carry out the most shocking act of genocide in human history

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Brodie Castle in Moray, and meets a family of bagpipe makers. Treasures include a cane with a concealed compass and telescope, an engraved silver salver awarded to a local engineer, a pair of rare 18th-century silk shoes, and a watch that saved someones life. Plus, the story of a sheep that changed sciences understanding of genetics

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce foxtrots with Anton Du Beke and explores a collection of clocks in the grounds of Belmont House in Kent. Treasures include a piece of HMS Bounty, a pair of bronze owls with some surprising contents, and a silver honey pot. Plus, Runjeet Singh explores newly discovered photographic archive revealing the story of Sikh worship in this corner of Kent

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Its the pursuit of cute as the team re-commission a 1991 Nissan Figaro

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team take on one of their most ambitious projects to date - a 1963 Lotus Elan Series 1. Their second project - a 90s BMW 3 series

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How the myth of German invincibility was finally debunked by their defeat at the Battle of Stalingrad. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: True Evil: The Making of a Nazi
programme.desc: Heinrich Himmler, the book-loving loner whose strange mystical beliefs drove him to carry out the most shocking act of genocide in human history

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Brodie Castle in Moray, and meets a family of bagpipe makers. Treasures include a cane with a concealed compass and telescope, an engraved silver salver awarded to a local engineer, a pair of rare 18th-century silk shoes, and a watch that saved someones life. Plus, the story of a sheep that changed sciences understanding of genetics

programme.title: Australia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The author and adventurer continues his Australian journey in Kakadu, the countrys largest national park, where he works through the night with a team committed to culling the poisonous feral cane toads that are threatening the regions wildlife. He then learns how to survive off the land using ancient Aboriginal knowledge as he heads out on patrol with a unique military unit in the remote northern Bush, before attempting to catch box jellyfish on Cape York Peninsula with a group of scientists who believe the species deadly venom may have medicinal qualities. Finally, Simon flies 100 miles out over the ocean to watch a large tanker being guided through the Great Barrier Reef

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Port Augusta to Darwin: The Ghan
programme.desc: Michael Portillo ventures Down Under to explore Australia by train. He begins by riding the legendary Ghan Railway, a train almost a kilometre in length and hauled by two locomotives, which covers nearly 2,000 miles across Australias red centre. Michael traces its route from the southern harbour of Port Augusta to Darwin on the north coast, along the way meeting the indigenous people of Alice Springs, visiting a kangaroo sanctuary and joining Australians to mark the anniversary of Anzac Day, the First Word War battle that defined their nation

programme.title: Incredible Journeys with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: A look at the challenge of preserving unspoilt wildernesses, from the icy expanses of Siberia to the tropical forests of central and South America. Simon reflects on how his past series have dealt with the causes and impact of climate change, as well as remembering a memorable report exposing the extent of plastic pollution in the ocean. He also recalls meeting indigenous people around the world and hearing their first-hand experiences of ecological damage

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Its the pursuit of cute as the team re-commission a 1991 Nissan Figaro

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Can the team finally smell profit when they sniff out a bargain Porsche? A DIY job on a Fiat should also keep the accountant happy but will it go to plan?

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jacks drawn the short straw again as he attempts to shift a couple of 1930s Austin Sevens that have been stuck in the same spot for half a century

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien hope a clay pigeon trap, a searchlight and a Peugeot 205 will help make a profit for their owners

programme.title: American Historys Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian reveals the myths and deceptions told following the United States emergence as a superpower after the Second World War. The 1950s and early 1960s in America is remembered as a golden era of abundance, harmony and the American dream made real. Lucy Worsley reveals that to have been a carefully constructed illusion and that the era of Americas supremacy was a time of government deception, racial conflict and fears of nuclear annihilation

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his exploration of Scotlands spectacular west coast from the Firth of Clyde to the Outer Hebrides. From the 250-year old new town of Helensburgh on the north bank of the Clyde, Michael takes his favourite West Highland Line across epic landscapes to Garelochhead and Ben Cruachan, and makes his way to Connel Bridge and the waters of Loch Etive

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Onboard the West Highland Line, Michael Portillo explores the stunning scenery of Scotlands west coast and islands as he heads to the Hebridean archipelago from the ferry terminal of Oban. On his way to the Isle of Mull, Michael watches eagerly for sea mammals in a stretch of water where many species of whales, dolphins and porpoises have been recorded, before checking out the daily catch in the fishing port of Tobermory. Later, Michael heads to the tiny Isle of Staffa, where he follows in the wake of a young Felix Mendelssohn, who was only 20-year-old when he set eyes on the volcanic sea cavern, Fingals Cave

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillos adventure reaches the west coast of Scotland where, aboard the Jacobite Express, he crosses the awe-inspiring Glenfinnan Viaduct. With his view of the curve of 21 arches built entirely of concrete, Michael and his fellow passengers are the envy of the crowds waving below. Spectacular engineering is in store for Michael in the Grampian mountains at Fort William, where he investigates the Lochaber Aluminium Works

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Mallaig to Isle of Lewis and Harris
programme.desc: Michael reaches the terminus of the West Highland Line at Mallaig, from where he follows in the footsteps of Bonnie Prince Charlie, over the sea to Skye. The island is renowned for wildlife and Michael seeks out one of its most spectacular residents, the sea eagle. He is lucky enough also to spot a school of dolphins

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Doncaster to collect four vintage BSAs motorbikes including one said to be the rarest BSA of all

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek makes the short trip to a mysterious Yorkshire beauty spot to pick up a 1920s style bus that isnt all that it seems

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How German U-boats were used to disrupt the flow of goods from America across the Atlantic Ocean in an attempt to starve Britain into submission

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Pauls on the East Coast to help a collector who is saying goodbye to his prized Excelsior Consort and his handmade Yorkshire Coble boat

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave Mathewson gets wind of a possible free lunch at the officers mess when he heads off to collect a stylish Swedish Volvo P1800S from an RAF base in the Midlands

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson has sold everything from trucks to bicycles - but he cant remember the last time he auctioned off a two-seater plane

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The latest stage of Michael Portillos trek takes him past lochs and castles on the Far North Line to Britains northernmost railway station at Thurso. From Lairg on the banks of Loch Shin, Michael heads for a secret conservation area in the 18-mile-long loch to track down one of Scotlands most elusive and endangered species, the freshwater pearl mussel

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: From Britains northernmost station at Thurso in Caithness, Michael Portillo heads for the ferry port of Scrabster to cross the Pentland Firth to the Orkney Islands. Passing the Old Man of Hoy, Michael takes in stunning views of the Orkney archipelago and the rugged coastlines of its 70 islands

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off from Tynemouth on the first leg of a trip to Berwick-upon-Tweed, hearing about of a 19th-century tragedy at sea that inspired 140 local men to set up a coastal rescue service. At Long Sands beach, Michael rolls up his sleeves to help volunteers caring for sick and injured seals at the Tynemouth Aquarium Seal Hospital

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads from Newcastle to Lynemouth, and is intrigued to hear of its part in Newcastles early industrial development. En route, Michael travels through the huge Northumberland coalfield and, at Cramlington, he alights to visit an extraordinary work of landform art made from the spoil of a coalmine, the vast Northumberlandia, known as the Lady of the North

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The drop in morale of Churchills forces following the fall of France to Nazi forces during the Second World War, and how the Dunkirk evacuation and the Battle of Britain helped to restore British confidence. Plus, Americas reasons for initially refusing to enter the conflict

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his exploration of Scotlands spectacular west coast from the Firth of Clyde to the Outer Hebrides. From the 250-year old new town of Helensburgh on the north bank of the Clyde, Michael takes his favourite West Highland Line across epic landscapes to Garelochhead and Ben Cruachan, and makes his way to Connel Bridge and the waters of Loch Etive

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Onboard the West Highland Line, Michael Portillo explores the stunning scenery of Scotlands west coast and islands as he heads to the Hebridean archipelago from the ferry terminal of Oban. On his way to the Isle of Mull, Michael watches eagerly for sea mammals in a stretch of water where many species of whales, dolphins and porpoises have been recorded, before checking out the daily catch in the fishing port of Tobermory. Later, Michael heads to the tiny Isle of Staffa, where he follows in the wake of a young Felix Mendelssohn, who was only 20-year-old when he set eyes on the volcanic sea cavern, Fingals Cave

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillos adventure reaches the west coast of Scotland where, aboard the Jacobite Express, he crosses the awe-inspiring Glenfinnan Viaduct. With his view of the curve of 21 arches built entirely of concrete, Michael and his fellow passengers are the envy of the crowds waving below. Spectacular engineering is in store for Michael in the Grampian mountains at Fort William, where he investigates the Lochaber Aluminium Works

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Mallaig to Isle of Lewis and Harris
programme.desc: Michael reaches the terminus of the West Highland Line at Mallaig, from where he follows in the footsteps of Bonnie Prince Charlie, over the sea to Skye. The island is renowned for wildlife and Michael seeks out one of its most spectacular residents, the sea eagle. He is lucky enough also to spot a school of dolphins

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Doncaster to collect four vintage BSAs motorbikes including one said to be the rarest BSA of all

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: A Year on Planet Earth
programme.desc: Stephen Fry looks at autumn in the northern hemisphere and spring in the south, seasons of change that bring opportunities, but also huge challenges. By September temperatures in northern Canada can plummet as low as -20C, but there is one stretch of river that still flows and bears are able to fish for the final salmon of the season. In southern Africa the rains have been gone for many months and watering holes have all-but dried up, while a king penguin chick on Marion Island answers the call of the ocean

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul helps out with dogs who have a variety of issues, including a pair of old labradors that cannot bear to be apart for one second, a Staffy that hates being left alone and a tiny puppy with an anti-social behaviour problem

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: The hospital staff have an intriguing case on their hands when local tramp Sebastian Fox is found dead. Ken Hopkirk rummages through his belongings and is surprised to find war medals, leading to the discovery that he was a decorated Navy hero. When a relative is traced but refuses to help with funeral arrangements, the Royal team decide to give him a fitting send-off by scattering his ashes at sea. Meanwhile, Jeffrey Goodwin is shocked to discover a farmers cattle herd have contracted a disease which has passed into cheese thats available in the area, and Jill Weatherill, Matron and Sister Brigid encounter a runaway girl. Rural medical drama, starring Michael Starke and Wendy Craig

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Dr Ormerod is furious to discover the police have launched an investigation into his wifes death, and reveals in a heated outburst that she was planning to leave him at the time of the accident. Romance blossoms for Dr Goodwin and student nurse Samantha Beaumont, and Lizzie Kennoways driving lesson ends in disaster when she slams into the back of Mr Harpers parked car. Rural medical drama, starring Robert Daws, Simon Molloy, Amy Robbins and Paul Fox

If the contestants meet expectations they will get the money, but fall short and the cash is split between the other teams

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi is called to the Gipsy Hill Hotel in Exeter, where owner Grace has plans to retire and hand the business to her daughter Jackie, who currently manages the hotel. But profits are falling and Grace worries there will be no business to pass down. Alex immediately spots that the hotel is stuck in the past, with its old-fashioned decor. Can she convince the owner to release her grip on the purse strings and allow her daughter to take ownership now?

programme.title: My Hoarder Mum and Me
programme.desc: A Place in the Sun presenter Jasmine Harman embarks on a personal journey to help her mother Vasoulla overcome her extreme hoarding habit. Over the course of 30 years, the familys five-bedroom London home has become filled from floor to ceiling with clutter, forcing Jasmines 12-year-old brother Cameron to move out. Debate still rages over whether hoarding should be classified as a mental illness, and Jasmine meets the families of other sufferers to learn how they deal with the problem. Narrated by Rupert Houseman

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The construction of Kansai International Airport upon the largest man-made island in the world, in Osaka Bay, Japan. Pioneering innovators from the past such as Pierre Danel, who developed a system against the pounding sea, and 19th-century mechanic Richard Dudgeon made the project possible. The development of airports including Berlins Tempelhof Airport and Londons Croydon Airport are also explored to reveal how engineers attempted this feat of engineering.

programme.title: This Time Next Year
programme.desc: Davina McCall meets a lady who wants to be able to smile for the first time in 30 years, and a music teacher who would love to become a published author. Plus, a schoolboy with dreams of becoming a professional footballer, and a young father desperate to thank the kind stranger who saved his life

programme.title: Danny Dyer: How to Be a Man
programme.desc: Danny Dyer travels the country exploring the contentious subject of modern masculinity, questioning whether men have lost their sense of identity and what it means to be male in 21st-century Britain. Along the way, he tries to get to the bottom of toxic masculinity and find out why so many young men are struggling with their mental health and relationships with women. Danny sets out to find out whether theres a rise in toxic masculinity in society and, if there is, why? He meets up with his brother Tony, before tackling controversial influencer Ed Matthews, meeting teenage boys at a London school, and speaking with Mansfield MP Ben Bradley, whos a champion of mens issues. He also meets male victims of domestic abuse, and in Brighton, he chats to members of the Brighton Gay Mens Chorus about overcoming prejudice

programme.title: Inside Monaco: Playground of the Rich
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix narrates a documentary giving an insight into the principality on the French Riviera, with access to the royal palace as well as the super-rich and those who serve them. In the first edition, director Michael Waldman explores how this extraordinary country operates, meeting everyone from Prince Albert and his staff to wealthy widow Monika Bacardi, who takes him on a tour in her chauffeur-driven classic Rolls-Royce. He also goes track side with ex-Formula One driver David Coulthard and team owner Eddie Jordan during the Monaco Grand Prix

programme.title: Body in the Bin: The Murder of Louise Kam
programme.desc: What began as an audacious plot to defraud £5million from unsuspecting 71-year-old mother and businesswoman Louise Kam ended in her murder, after which her body was callously dumped in a wheelie bin on a driveway belonging to one of the killers. Pioneering innovators from the past such as Pierre Danel, who developed a system against the pounding sea, and 19th-century mechanic Richard Dudgeon made the project possible. The development of airports including Berlins Tempelhof Airport and Londons Croydon Airport are also explored to reveal how engineers attempted this feat of engineering.

programme.title: This Time Next Year
programme.desc: Davina McCall meets a lady who wants to be able to smile for the first time in 30 years, and a music teacher who would love to become a published author. Plus, a schoolboy with dreams of becoming a professional footballer, and a young father desperate to thank the kind stranger who saved his life

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Staff are gearing up for a weekend of fun and screams as Halloween arrives. At the Flamingo, Michael is on reception with a busy day of checking in guests, while the maintenance team deals with a burst pipe. Entertainment is key to Halloween and Shari is frantically getting the decorations up, while youngster Tom is entertaining the guests

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Ireland AM is the longest running daytime show in Ireland. Blending a mixture of lifestyle interviews and news content, with gardening, health, food and fashion seven days a week

programme.title: John and Lisas Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare the perfect meal for a special night in, with a starter of scallops served with crispy bacon and a cauliflower puree. The main course is roast chicken stuffed with truffle butter with dauphinoise potatoes and charred cabbage, followed by and a chocolate and almond tart with amaretti biscuits for dessert

programme.title: Heathrow: Britains Busiest Airport
programme.desc: The Red Arrows fly over the airport during the Queens Jubilee celebrations, but Passenger Experience Manager Suki has to wake a passenger and get him onboard a flight to Toronto. Also racing to catch their flight are a mother and daughter flying to South Africa to attend a close family relatives funeral. Emotions run high as time ticks away without the correct paperwork, so its down to Passenger Experience Manager Harry to come to the rescue - but is it already too late?

programme.title: Rookie Nurses
programme.desc: An emergency call comes in to the stroke unit at Bronglais Hospital, Aberystwyth. Issie is becoming more confident in helping stroke patients when they arrive, but is quickly reminded she has a lot to learn and experience to gain. A man is blue-lighted in to AandE after being knocked off his bike, while Caitlin deals with a patient who has been admitted after having seizures

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges. Bizarre new game Flush or Float is showcased, before the host takes a trip to Milton Keynes to give away £1,000 via In for a Penny, In for a Grand

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts as Jon Sopel, Maisie Summers-Newton, Perri Shakes-Drayton and Brian Conley pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Britains Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host as the auditions continue, with more acts taking to the stage with performances to impress the crowd and judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and KSI. Which acts will win themselves a coveted space in the semi-finals and the chance of a prize of £250,000 and a sought-after slot at the Royal Variety Performance.

programme.title: Me and My Tourettes
programme.desc: Documentary following three people with Tourettes syndrome as they reach turning points in their lives at a camp that could change everything for them and their families

programme.title: Danny Dyer: How to Be a Man
programme.desc: Danny Dyer travels the country exploring the contentious subject of modern masculinity, questioning whether men have lost their sense of identity and what it means to be male in 21st-century Britain. Along the way, he tries to get to the bottom of toxic masculinity and find out why so many young men are struggling with their mental health and relationships with women. Danny sets out to find out whether theres a rise in toxic masculinity in society and, if there is, why? He meets up with his brother Tony, before tackling controversial influencer Ed Matthews, meeting teenage boys at a London school, and speaking with Mansfield MP Ben Bradley, whos a champion of mens issues. He also meets male victims of domestic abuse, and in Brighton, he chats to members of the Brighton Gay Mens Chorus about overcoming prejudice

programme.title: The Big Interview
programme.desc: Colette Fitzpatrick meets Kathleen Chada, whose husband murdered her young sons Eoghan and Ruairi in 2013

If the contestants meet expectations they will get the money, but fall short and the cash is split between the other teams

programme.title: Body in the Bin: The Murder of Louise Kam
programme.desc: What began as an audacious plot to defraud £5million from unsuspecting 71-year-old mother and businesswoman Louise Kam ended in her murder, after which her body was callously dumped in a wheelie bin on a driveway belonging to one of the killers. This documentary follows this shocking story of fraud, murder and the impact of social media

programme.desc: The unit investigates the links between a crime lord and a deranged artist who may have been using his skills to create something illegal. Crime drama, starring Jason Beghe and Sophia Bush

programme.title: The Chase Australia

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Conclusion of a two-part storyline. A kidnap case leaves Vernons plans for Liz and Bens wedding in chaos, while Helens dreams of a fresh start for her and Matt look set to be shattered. Jenny is relieved when she finally gets a medical diagnosis explaining her recent symptoms, but the news brings a difficult decision for husband Dennis, who has to choose between his wife and his police career. Starring newcomer Sophie Ward, Oliver Montgomery, Duncan Bell and Sarah Tansey

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Veteran singer and keyboard player Alan Price guest stars in this festive episode of the rural drama, as the leader of a jazz band. Travelling to Aidensfield, where theyve been booked by Vernon to play at the villagers Christmas concert, the Rio Trio offer a lift to a girl, unaware she is a single mum taking her baby home to see her parents for the holiday. However, the first the band know about the infant is when the girl disappears, leaving them to look after it. Meanwhile, as the concert gets into full swing, local barmaid Gina takes centre stage, hoping to impress all and sundry with her rendition of Winter Wonderland. Tricia Penrose, Derek Fowlds, Geoffrey Hughes, William Simons and Mark Jordon star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Walker is forced to confront his past when he undertakes his own unofficial investigation into a series of violent robberies around Aidensfield. Helen has to come to terms with the fact that her marriage is probably over when Matthews mistress appears in the village, and David finds himself involved in some shady behaviour. Jonathan Kerrigan, Sophie Ward, Oliver Montgomery and David Lonsdale star

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Egyptian Tomb
programme.desc: An archaeologist dies of a heart attack shortly after smashing the seal of an ancient Egyptian tomb, raising the spectre of the mummys curse. The grieving widow summons the Belgian sleuth to unravel the mystery, but he can only smoke out the real villain by risking his own life. David Suchet stars, with Anna Cropper and Bill Bailey

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victims widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ben forces Maggie to reveal that Alan was not his father - but can she keep Jim McDonald a secret? Meanwhile, Debbie encounters Carl upon her release from custody, and Sam reluctantly accepts Wills apology

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ronnie learns of Carls latest ploy to extort cash from Debbie, Ben reels from the paternity revelation, Jodie joins David on his day off, and Hope taunts Will in front of Sam

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Debbie makes her presence felt at the hotel, Sam accuses Will of intimidation, Ben refuses to give Maggie the time of day, and Tim summons the courage to face Trisha

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Summer challenges Debbies version of how Billy died, Will denies Sams allegation that hes still seeing Megan, and Lisa and Carla leave Jodie in charge of Connie

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sarah sneaks round to see Todd while Theo is out, Jodie reveals her mischief to Gemma, Nick and Ben decide to get their sons together, and Ronnie offers Carl an olive branch

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sarah quizzes Danielle about Theos violent streak, Bernie hears promising news of Mals future plans, George meets an estate agent, and Carl gives Summer a clue to the identity of Billys killer

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective is assigned to investigate when a chocolate factory owner is murdered during a fox hunt. The case leads him to the sleepy Oxfordshire village of Chigton Green, and is soon complicated by the fatal shooting of a local couple with an apparent connection to the first victim. Endeavour uncovers a vicious campaign of gossip and rumour, and meets a local woman who inspires him to consider his future

programme.title: Building Giants
programme.desc: The construction of the Rose Fitzgerald Kennedy Bridge over the River Barrow in Co Kilkenny, which has two spans of more than 750ft and stands on a purpose-built artificial island

programme.desc: Robson Green and Jim Murray head to Scotland where they fish at the River Deveron before heading to the most famous river of them all: the Spey
